---
title: "Supplementary material for 'Statistical tests for bivariate spatial association across multi-omics data with disjoint coordinates'"
author: "Stijn Hawinkel, Wangjun Hu, Britta Velten and Steven Maere"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: true
    keep_tex: yes
    includes:
            in_header: stxRes.sty
---

\beginsupplement{0}

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  cache = TRUE, autodep = TRUE, warning = FALSE, dpi = 125,
  cache.lazy = FALSE, message = FALSE, echo = FALSE,
  eval = TRUE, tidy = TRUE, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5,
  purl = TRUE, fig.pos = "p"
)
libs <- c(
  "ggplot2", "reshape2", "xtable", "knitr", "gplots", "openxlsx", "patchwork",
  "parallel", "dplyr", "Matrix", "RANN", "SpatialPack", "stringr", "mvtnorm", "spdep", "ggh4x",
  "nlme", "NTSS", "peakRAM", "gstat", "SpaGene", "rhdf5", "anndata", "Cardinal", "sbivar", "Seurat", "smoppix", "fdrtool", "nleqslv", "httr", "bispdep", "jsonlite", "spatstat", "lmerTest", "abind"
)
for (i in libs) {
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(i, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE))
}
rm(i, libs)
for (i in list.files("R")) {
  source(file.path("R", i))
}
rm(i)
theme_set(theme_bw())
sigLevel <- 0.05
nCores <- 2
register(MulticoreParam(nCores))
# Create folders to store the graphs, data and results
foo <- lapply(c("GraphsPaper", "Results", "Data", "Data/Godfrey2025", "Data/Vicari2024", "Data/Vicari2024/Stx", "Results/landmarks/"), dir.nw)
```

```{r defineLabels}
spatPats <- selfName(c("none", "singleGradient", "orthogonalGradient", "independentGPs", "independentZones", "independentStreak", "dependentGPs", "dependentGPsNeg", "dependentGPsnoSAC", "sharedGradient", "oppositeGradient", "dependentStreak", "oppositeStreak", "dependentZones", "oppositeZones", "dependentStreakGPs", "dependentZonesGPs"))
spatPatsShow <- selfName(c("none", "independentGPs", "dependentGPs", "dependentZones", "dependentStreak", "dependentStreakGPs", "dependentZonesGPs"))
spatPatsLabels <- c("No spatial pattern", "Single gradient", "Orthogonal gradients", "Independent GPs", "Independent zones", "Perpendicular streak", "Dependent GPs", "Depedent GPs\n(negative)", "Dependent GPs\n(no SAC)", "Shared gradient", "Opposite gradient", "Streak", "Opposite streak", "Zones", "Opposite zones", "Streak + GPs", "Zones + GPs")
spatPatsShowLabels <- spatPatsLabels[match(spatPatsShow, spatPats)]
powerColours <- c("chartreuse3", "darkgreen", "black", "aquamarine3", "orange", "bisque", "darkslategray4", "bisque3", "cyan", "grey25", "magenta", "pink", "darkgoldenrod3", "brown", "red", "blue", "darkorchid3")
methLevels <- c("MoransI_Czaplewski", "MoransI_permutation", "MoransI_SpatialDM", "MoransI_RandomShift", "LeesL_asymptotic", "LeesL_permutation", "GearysC_permutation", "GearysC_randomisation", "SpaGene", "SpaceBF", "PearsonCor", "modTtest", "RandomShift", "MoransI_sbivar", "GP", "GAM", "GAMM")
methLabels <- c("Moran's I (randomisation)", "Moran's I (permutation)", "Moran's I (SpatialDM)", "Moran's I (random shift)", "Lee's L (randomisation)", "Lee's L (permutation)", "Geary's C (permutation)", "Geary's C (randomisation)", "SpaGene", "SpaceBF", "Pearson correlation", "Modified t-test", "Kendall's correlation\n(random shift)", "Moran's I (independence) [sbivar]", "Bivariate GPs [sbivar]", "GAMs [sbivar]", "GAMMs [sbivar]")
methLabelsShow <- c("Moran's I (randomisation)", "Moran's I (permutation)", "Moran's I (SpatialDM)", "Moran's I (random shift)", "SpaGene", "SpaceBF", "Lee's L (randomisation)", "Geary's C (permutation)", "Pearson correlation", "Modified t-test", "Kendall's correlation\n(random shift)", "Moran's I (independence) [sbivar]", "Bivariate GPs [sbivar]", "GAMs [sbivar]", "GAMMs [sbivar]")
names(powerColours) <- methLabels
scrambleLabels <- c("Transcriptomics", "Metabolomics", "Transcriptomics +\nMetabolomics")
scrambleVec <- selfName(c("Stx", "Met", "Stx_Met"))
timeLabels <- c("Runtime (in seconds)", "Peak RAM usage (in MB)")
timeLevels <- c("Elapsed_Time_sec", "Peak_RAM_Used_MiB")
```

\tableofcontents

# Algorithms

## Existing algorithms

### Bivariate Moran's I \label{sec:moransI}

The bivariate Moran's I is defined for two vectors $\mb x$ and $\mb y$ as $I_{xy} = \sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^mw_{ij}x_iy_j = \mb x^t \mb W \mb y$. When testing only for spatial association between spots, under the randomisation null hypothesis for joint coordinate sets, $I_{xy}$ has an expected value of $E(I_{xy}) = \frac{\Cor{x, y}}{(n-1)}$ \parencite{Czaplewski1993}. If zero correlation within the same spots is also considered part of the null hypothesis, as we do throughout the article, than the expectation equals 0. Several versions of the variance of bivariate Moran's I exist for the case of joint coordinate sets. \textcite{Czaplewski1993} provides the variance under the randomisation null hypothesis. \textcite{Li2023} provides a variance assuming no SAC in either outcome variable in their \textit{SpatialDM} method, as do \textcite{DeTomaso2021}. \textcite{Miller2021} employ the bivariate Moran's I in their \textit{MERINGUE} method, using permutations to determine significance, as does \textcite{Wartenberg1985}.

### The modified t-test \label{sec:modTtest}

Pearson correlation after spot matching or aggregation is commonly used to test for spatial association in multi-omics studies \parencite{Vicari2024, Godfrey2025, Ravi2022}, although it is known that such test must account for SAC to be valid \parencite{Lennon2000}. The most common correction is the modified t-test, correcting the degrees of freedom of the t-test and thus the variance estimate of the Pearson correlation estimate $\hat{\rho}_{xy}$ based on SAC in the two variables \parencite{Clifford1989, Dutilleul1993, Dutilleul2008}. The variance of $\hat{\rho}_{xy}$ is given by $(n-1)^{-2}tr(\bs\Sigma_x\bs\Sigma_y)$, with $\bs\Sigma_x$ and $\bs\Sigma_y$ the spatial autocorrelation matrices of *x* and *y*, respectively, and $tr(.)$ the trace function \parencite{Dutilleul2008,Clifford1989}.

### Other measures of spatial association

SpaGene by \parencite{Liu2022b} defines its own measure of spatial colocalisation based on k-nearest neighbour networks, and determines significance through permutations. \textcite{Seal2026} notice problems with autocorrelation in simulations, and present their method SpaceBF to account for SAC in a Bayesian framework. Yet we found this method too slow to be applied in our simulations or on real spatial omics data. \textcite{Lee2001} presents Lee's L as measure of spatial association for joint coordinate sets. \textcite{Bivand2018} generalised Geary's C for the bivariate case of joint coordinate sets. To determine significance for any test statistic while accounting for SAC, custom permutation schemes have been developed that test the independence null hypothesis: the so-called "random-shift" tests \parencite{Mrkvicka2021, Ridder2024}. These involve shifting both images with respect to each other, which retains SAC, calculating the association measure based on the regions that still overlap, and correcting their variance for the reduction in overlap area.

## New algorithms

### Bivariate Moran's I under the independence null hypothesis \label{sec:MoransI}

#### Expectation

Under the independence null hypothesis, implying no correlation within the same measurement spots, for both joint and disjoint coordinate sets, bivariate Moran's I has expectation zero:

\begin{equation}
\begin{aligned}
E(I_{xy}) &= E\left(\sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^mw_{ij}x_iy_j \right)\\
&= \sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^mw_{ij}E\left(x_iy_j \right)=0
\end{aligned}
\label{eq:expMoran}
\end{equation}

#### Maximum absolute value

The lower and upper bounds of the bivariate Moran's I statistic are dependent on the weight matrix and usually not -1 and 1. Instead, they depend on the largest singular value of the weight matrix as follows. The problem is to find **x** and **y** that maximize $I_{xy} = \mb x^t \mb W \mb y$ for fixed **W** and subject to $\mb x^t\mb x = n-1$ and $\mb y^t\mb y = m-1$. Given the singular value decomposition $\mb U \Upsilon \mb V^t$ of **W**, with $r=\text{min}(n,m)$ and $\Upsilon$ the diagonal matrix of singular values, this amounts to optimizing $\mb x^t \mb W \mb y = \mb x^t \mb U \Upsilon \mb V^t \mb y$. The columns of **U** and **V** being orthonormal, and $\upsilon_1 \geq \upsilon_2 \geq \hdots\geq \upsilon_r$, this measure is optimized when $\mb x$ aligns perfectly with the first column of **U** ($\mb u_1$), and $\mb y$ with the first column of **V** ($\mb v_1$), achieving $\sqrt{(n-1)(m-1)}\upsilon_1$ as maximum value. By symmetry, the minimum is achieved when $\mb x = -\mb u_1$ and $\mb y = \mb v_1$ (or $\mb x = \mb u_1$ and $\mb y = -\mb v_1$), which is $-\sqrt{(n-1)(m-1)}\upsilon_1$. It may be useful to scale $I_{xy}$ values by their maximum absolute value $\sqrt{(n-1)(m-1)}\upsilon_1$ to render them comparable across sections with different measurements grids and corresponding weight matrices.

#### Variance

The expectation and maximum absolute value are the same under the randomisation and independence null hypotheses, but the variance differs as it is affected by SAC, as derived in \eqref{eq:varMoranNum} in the main paper. We estimate the SAC matrices nonparametrically using Matheron’s variogram estimator \parencite{Matheron1963} following \textcite{Dutilleul2008}. Next we fit linear and exponential functions through the estimate variogram using the \textit{fit.variogram} function in the \textit{gstat} R-package \cite{Pebesma2004}, fixing the sill to 1, and retain the fits ($\hat{\sigma}_x$ and $\hat{\sigma}_y$) with the smallest mean squared error loss. Then we plug the covariance as a function of distance $\hat{\sigma}_x(d_{ij})$ and $\hat{\sigma}_y(d_{kl})$ into \eqref{eq:varMoranNum} in the main paper, to yield $\widehat{\text{Var}}(I_{xy}) = \sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^m\sum_{k=1}^n\sum_{l=1}^mw_{ij}w_{kl}\hat{\sigma}_x(d_{ij})\hat{\sigma}_y(d_{kl})$. This equals $\widehat{\text{Var}}(I_{xy}) = tr(\mb W^t \bs \Sigma_x \mb W \bs \Sigma_y)$, with $\Sigma_x$ and $\Sigma_x$ the autocovariance matrices built by evaluating the covariance functions in the observed distance matrices. The formula for joint coordinate sets is the same; we do not distinguish between association within the same coordinates and between nearby coordinates, the former ones simply get the highest weight.

#### Empirical distribution \label{sec:empDistr}

For significance testing on $I_{xy}$ we the assume that $\frac{I_{xy}}{\sqrt{\widehat{\text{Var}}(I_{xy})}}$ is asymptotically standard normal. This is justified by the current multi-omics technologies generating thousands of spots per image, and by theoretical results on univariate Moran's I \parencite{Kelejian2001}. We verify this assumption empirically in a small simulation study in the 'none' scenario from the parametric simulations with disjoint coordinate sets, revealing approximate normality of the statistic (\fref{fig:qqnormMoran}).

```{r parSetup}
xMin <- 0
xMax <- 1
nSims <- 5e1
nPerms <- 3e2
corVals <- c(1, 0.25)
csGaus <- corGaus(form = ~ x + y, nugget = TRUE, value = corVals) # Gaussian correlation structure
optControl <- nlme::lmeControl(
  opt = "optim", maxIter = 5e2, msMaxIter = 5e2,
  niterEM = 1e3, msMaxEval = 1e3
)
Nugs <- c(0.25, 0.25)
Rangs <- c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15)
Sigs <- c(0.75, 0.75, 0.15)
samSizesJoint <- c(20^2, 25^2, 30^2, 35^2, 40^2, 45^2)
nFeats <- 10
jointGrid <- expand.grid(stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "spatPats" = spatPatsShow, "rep" = seq_len(nSims))
featGrid <- expand.grid("i" = seq_len(nFeats), "j" = seq_len(nFeats))
propFeatNull <- 0.2
```

```{r bivMoranDistr, dpi = 300, fig.cap = "Normal QQ-plot of 10,000 bivariate Moran's I statistics generated under the 'none' scenario with $\\eta=0.0001$.\\label{fig:qqnormMoran}", fig.width = 3.5, fig.height = 4}
n <- 400
m <- 900
xMat <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
yMat <- makeCoordGrid(m, xMin + 0.03, xMax + 0.03)
wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(xMat, yMat, wo = "Gauss", eta = 1e-4)
Ixys <- vapply(integer(1e4), FUN.VALUE = 0, function(i) {
  c(scale(rnorm(n))) %*% wm %*% c(scale(rnorm(m)))
})
qqnorm(Ixys, main = "", cex = 0.6)
qqline(Ixys)
rm(wm, Ixys)
```

```{r plotMoranPaper, include = FALSE}
n_low <- 4^2
n_high <- 7^2
tmp <- seq(0.05, 1, length.out = sqrt(n_high))
high_res <- expand.grid(x = tmp, y = tmp)
high_res$tech <- "High-resolution"
n_low <- n_high / 4
low_res <- data.frame(
  x = seq(0.075, 0.925, length.out = sqrt(n_low)),
  y = seq(0.075, 0.925, length.out = sqrt(n_low))
)
low_res <- expand.grid(x = low_res$x, y = low_res$y)
low_res$tech <- "Low-resolution"
low_res$x <- low_res$x + 0.01 
low_res$y <- low_res$y + 0.02
df <- rbind(high_res, low_res)
target <- low_res[aaa <- 13, , drop = FALSE]
distances <- sqrt((high_res$x - target$x)^2 + (high_res$y - target$y)^2)
decay_param <- 5.8 
weights <- exp(-decay_param * distances)
high_res <- high_res %>%
  mutate(weight = weights / sum(weights))
segments <- high_res %>%
  mutate(
    x_start = target$x,
    y_start = target$y
  )
pMoran <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = segments,
    aes(x = x_start, y = y_start, xend = x, yend = y, linewidth = weight, alpha = weight),
    color = "gray30"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = high_res,
    aes(x, y),
    color = "blue",
    size = 1.2,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = target,
    aes(x, y),
    shape = 21, alpha = 0.6,
    fill = "red",
    color = "black",
    size = 3.1,
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = low_res[-aaa, ],
    aes(x, y),
    shape = 21, alpha = 0.2,
    fill = "red",
    color = "black",
    size = 3.1,
    stroke = 0.1
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(0.1, 2.5), guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.05, 0.8), guide = "none") +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(x = "Coordinate 1", y = "Coordinate 2") +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 11.2), axis.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
ggsave(pMoran, file = "GraphsPaper/plotMoran.pdf", width = 2.25, height = 2.25)
rm(pMoran, segments, df, high_res, low_res, newGrid)
```

#### The weight function \label{sec:weightFun}

The choice of weight matrix **W** determines the power of tests based on bivariate Moran's I. The three different weight functions tested by default, for coordinate matrices shifted and scaled to the unit square with corners $(0,0)^t$, $(0,1)^t$, $(1,1)^t$ and $(1,0)^t$, are illustrated in \fref{fig:weight}. The p-values from the different weight matrices are combined into one p-value through the Cauchy combination test \parencite{Liu2022b}.

```{r weightFun, fig.cap = "Illustration of weighting functions used for constructing the weight matrix used for a number of association metrics, including bivariate Moran's I.\\label{fig:weight}", fig.height =3.5, fig.width = 5}
etas <- eval(formals(sbivar:::sbivarSingle)$etas)
exploreWeights(etas)
```

#### The random-shift null distribution \label{sec:MoranRandomShift}

As an alternative to our analytical derivation of the variance and normality assumption, we adapt the random shift tests by \textcite{Mrkvicka2021, Ridder2024} to the bivariate Moran's I, and approximate its null distribution under the independence null hypothesis as follows. First the coordinate matrices are moved and scaled to the unit square. The weight matrix and observed bivariate Moran's I are calculated as before. Next a point is drawn uniformly on a disc of radius 0.5 centered in the origin, defining a vector $\mb v$ from the origin to this point. Define a shifted window with corners $(0,0)^t+\mb v$, $(0,1)^t+\mb v$, $(1,1)^t+\mb v$ and $(1,0)^t+\mb v$, and find the subsets $\mb C_-$ and $\mb E_-$ of sizes $n_-$ and $m_-$ within the shifted window. Shift $\mb C_-$ in the reverse direction to obtain $\mb C^\ast_- = \mb C_- - \mb v$. Calculate bivariate Moran's I $I_{xy-}$ with coordinates $\mb C^\ast_-$ and $\mb E_-$ and corresponding observations, and scale it to $I_{xy-}\sqrt{n_-m_-}$ to account for the variance inflation due to subsetting. Repeat this procedure $B$ times by drawing more instances $\mb v$ on the disc to build a null distribution for the scaled bivariate Moran's I statistic $I_{xy}\sqrt{nm}$. Following \textcite{Phipson2010}, then calculate $Q = \frac{1+\sum_{b=1}^B I\left(I_{xy}\sqrt{nm} > I_{xy-}^b\sqrt{n^b_-m^b_-}\right)}{B+1}$ with $I(.)$ the indicator function, and the p-values as $2\times\text{min}(Q, 1-Q)$.

### Bivariate Gaussian processes (GPs) \label{sec:GP}

#### Derivation of the score test

The GP score test statistic *U* was defined in \eqref{eq:Ustat} in the main text. It tests the null hypothesis that $\tau$, the variance of the random effect engendering the bivariate association, equals 0. Under the null, *U* asymptotically follows a mixture of chi-squared distributions, which can be approximated using the Satterwaithe method by a scaled chi-squared distribution $\kappa \chi^2_\nu$ \parencite{Zhang2003, Kats2021}. Call $e = 0.5 tr\left(\mb P^{-1}\bs\Sigma^{biv}\right)$ and the Fisher information matrix $I_{\tau\tau} = 0.5tr\left((\mb P^{-1}\bs\Sigma^{biv})^2\right)$ with $\mb A$ the design matrix, $\mb P = \bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1} - \bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\mb A (\mb A^t \bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1} \mb A)^{-1}\mb A^t\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}$, then $\kappa = I_{\tau\tau}/(2e)$ and $\nu = 2e^2/I_{\tau\tau}$ \parencite{Kats2021, Zhang2003}. \textcite{Zhang2003} recommend to use the efficient information $\tilde{I}_{\tau\tau} = I_{\tau\tau} - I_{\tau\bs\theta}I_{\bs\theta\bs\theta}^{-1}I_{\tau\bs\theta}^t$ rather than $I_{\tau\tau}$ with $\bs\theta$ the vector of (co)variance parameters of length $g$ defining $\bs\Sigma_\ast$. For the typical geostatistics parametrisation, used for instance in the *gls* function in the *nlme* R-package \parencite{Pinheiro2000} this is $\bs\theta^t = (\sigma_x^2, \sigma_y^2, \pi_x, \pi_y, l_x, l_y)$ with $\sigma^2_x$ the variance, $\pi_x$ the nugget and $l_x$ the length scale of the x-variable, and analogously for the y-variable. Here we use the Gaussian covariance function $\Cov{x_i, x_j} = \sigma_x^2\left(\pi_xI(d_{ij}=0) + (1-\pi_x) \exp\left[-(\frac{d_{ij}}{l_x})^2\right]\right)$ with $d_{ij}$ the distance between observations $i$ and $j$. Further $I_{\tau\bs\theta} = 0.5tr(\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\bs\Sigma^{biv}\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial \bs\theta})$ and $I_{\bs\theta\bs\theta} = 0.5tr(\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial \bs\theta}\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial \bs\theta})$ \parencite{Zhang2003}. Unlike in the simple univariate case in e.g. *SpatialDE2* by \textcite{Kats2021} where $\bs\theta = \sigma^2_x$, here $\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial \bs\theta}$ is a $h\times h\times g$ tensor. The traces are here taken over the first two dimensions only, meaning the trace is applied over matrix slices of dimensions $h\times h$, such that $I_{\bs\theta\bs\theta}$ is a $g\times g$ matrix and $I_{\tau\bs\theta}$ a vector of length $g$. Also the matrix multiplications $\bs\Sigma_\ast^{-1}\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial \bs\theta}$ are done one matrix at a time to yield tensors again. We now find the derivatives $\frac{\partial\bs\Sigma_\ast}{\partial\bs\theta}$ for on-diagonal elements as

\begin{equation}
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ii}}{\partial\sigma_x} = 2\sigma_x\\
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ii}}{\partial\pi_x} = 0\\
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ii}}{\partial l_x} = 0,\\
\end{aligned}
\label{eq:diagGeo}
\end{equation}

and for off-diagonal elements

\begin{equation}
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ij}}{\partial\sigma_x} = 2\sigma_x \exp\left[-(d_{ij}/l_x)^2\right](1-\pi_x)\\
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ij}}{\partial\pi_x} = -\sigma_x^2\exp\left[-(d_{ij}/l_x)^2\right](1-\pi_x)\\
&\frac{\partial\Sigma_{\ast ij}}{\partial l_x} = 2\sigma_x^2(1-\pi_x)\exp\left[-(d_{ij}/l_x)^2\right]\frac{d_{ij}^2}{l_x^3}.
\end{aligned}
\label{eq:OffDiagGeo}
\end{equation}

Switching covariance kernels implies revisiting these expressions. The test is designed for univariate GPs estimated by restricted maximum likelihood (REML), e.g. with the *gls* function in the *nlme* package \parencite{Pinheiro2000}, which separates mean from covariance estimation. For speedup, one could instead use the *gpytorch* python package to fit the univariate GPs, on CPU as well as with GPU acceleration \parencite{Gardner2018}. It is then crucial not to estimate the mean freely as default in *gpytorch*, but instead fix it to the empirical mean to approximate the REML outcome. Yet for the final computation time the GPU acceleration is of little importance, as calculating the test statistic *U* and its variance takes up most of the computation time. Hence GPU acceleration is not included in the *sbivar* package.

```{r plotCovaMatsPaper, include = FALSE}
nPlot <- 20
mPlot <- 12
xMatPlot <- makeCoordGrid(nPlot, 0, 1)
yMatPlot <- makeCoordGrid(mPlot, 0, 1)
distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(xMatPlot, yMatPlot)))
hPlot <- nPlot + mPlot
rangeFactor <- 20
nugFactor <- 0.8
nullSigma <- bdiag(
  buildSigmaGp(c("range" = Rangs[1] * rangeFactor, "nugget" = Nugs[1] * nugFactor, "sigma" = Sigs[1]), distMat = as.matrix(dist(xMatPlot))),
  buildSigmaGp(c("range" = Rangs[2] * rangeFactor, "nugget" = Nugs[2] * nugFactor, "sigma" = Sigs[2]), distMat = as.matrix(dist(yMatPlot)))
)
lscAlt <- mean(Rangs) * rangeFactor
altSigma <- {
  tmpAlt2 <- sparseMatrix(i = rep(seq_len(nPlot), times = mPlot), j = rep(seq_len(mPlot) + nPlot, each = nPlot), dims = c(hPlot, hPlot), symmetric = TRUE, x = c(exp(-(distMat[seq_len(nPlot), seq_len(mPlot) + nPlot] / lscAlt)^2)))
  diag(tmpAlt2) <- 1
  tmpAlt2
}
pdf("GraphsPaper/covMats.pdf", width = 4.15, height = 1.7)
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(0.6, 0.6, 1.5, 0.6))
image(diag(hPlot)[rev(seq_len(hPlot)), ], main = "Independence", asp = 1, axes = FALSE)
image(as.matrix(nullSigma)[rev(seq_len(hPlot)), ], main = "SAC", asp = 1, axes = FALSE)
image(as.matrix(altSigma)[rev(seq_len(hPlot)), ], main = "Bivariate", asp = 1, axes = FALSE)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
dev.off()
rm(altSigma, nullSigma, tmpAlt2, xMatPlot, yMatPlot)
```

#### The score test for other outcome distributions

The score test can be generalized to other outcome distributions and link functions, although this is currently not implemented in the \sbivar package. Building on \textcite{Zhang2003, Kats2021}, \eqref{eq:Ustat} then becomes

\begin{equation}
U = 1/2\left((\mb z^\ast - \bs \mu)^t \bs\Delta \mb K^{-1} \bs\Sigma^{biv} \mb K^{-1} \bs\Delta(\mb z^\ast - \bs \mu)\right).
\label{eq:Ustatcount}
\end{equation}

For count distributions where the variance equals the mean, this becomes $\bs\Delta = \diag(g'(\bs\mu)) = \diag(\bs\mu)^{-1}$ and $\mb K = g'(\bs\mu) \bs\Sigma^\ast g'(\bs\mu)^t = \diag(\bs\mu)^{-1}$ and $g$ the link function. $\mb z^\ast = \frac{z-\bs\mu}{\bs\mu}$ is the working vector under the null.

### Generalized additive models (GAMs)\label{sec:GAMs}

We extend the bivariate test based on GAMs proposed in the main text to other outcome distributions and link functions. The models for the mean can then be written with the link functions $g$ and $h$ as

\begin{equation}
\begin{aligned}
g(E(x_i)) &= \beta_{0x} + s_x(\mb c_i) \\
h(E(y_j)) &= \beta_{0y} + s_y(\mb e_j).
\end{aligned}
\label{eq:splinesE}
\end{equation}

Call $\iota_i =s_x(\mb c_i)$ and $\zeta_j =s_x(\mb e_j)$. The covariance estimate is then $q_{xy} = (B-1)^{-1}\sum_{b=1}^B (\iota_b - \bar{\iota})(\zeta_b-\bar{\zeta})$, with gradient $\nabla q_{xy}^t = \left(\frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \iota_1}, \frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \iota_2}, \hdots, \frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \iota_B}, \frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \zeta_1}, \frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \zeta_2}, \hdots, \frac{\partial q_{xy}}{\partial \zeta_B}\right)$. The variance-covariance matrix of $\bs \iota$ is given by $\nabla \hat{\bs \iota}^t \bs\Phi_x \nabla \hat{\bs \iota}$ with $\nabla \hat{\bs \iota}^t= \left(\frac{\partial \hat{\bs \iota}}{\partial \beta_1}, \frac{\partial \hat{\bs \iota}}{\partial \beta_2}, \hdots, \frac{\partial\hat{\bs \iota}}{\partial \beta_h}\right)$, in which $\frac{\partial \hat{\bs \iota}}{\partial \beta_1} = (g^{-1})'(\beta_0 + \mb H_{x}\bs\beta_x + g_x(\mb c_i))\mb H_{1x}$ and $\bs\Phi_x$ the variance-covariance matrix of the thin plate spline coefficients $\bs\beta_x$. The variance of the correlation coefficient can be approximated by

\begin{equation}
\Var{\hat{q}_{xy}} \approx \nabla q_{xy}^t \nabla \hat{\mb z}^t \bs\Phi\nabla \hat{\mb z} \nabla q_{xy},
\label{eq:varLink}
\end{equation}

with $\bs\Phi$ a block diagonal matrix of $\bs\Phi_x$ and $\bs\Phi_y$, and $\hat{\mb z}^t = (\hat{\bs \iota}^t, \hat{\bs \zeta}^t)$. \eqref{eq:varLink} simplifies to \eqref{eq:varq} in the main text for the identity link.

```{r plotSplinesPaper, include = FALSE}
dat <- makeXY(xMat, yMat, nSims = 1, what = "dependentStreak", boundsBetas = c(3, 3))
colnames(dat$x)[1] <- "x"
colnames(dat$y)[1] <- "y"
plotGAMs(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat,
  n_points = 500, addTitle = FALSE,
  features = c("x", "y")
) +
  labs(x = "Coordinate 1", y = "Coordinate 2")
ggsave(file = "GraphsPaper/plotSplines.pdf", width = 6, height = 2.5)
rm(dat)
```

\clearpage

# Simulation studies

## Setup

\fref{fig:design} shows the grids at which outcomes are generated in the parametric simulation studies, \fref{fig:designs} illustrates the spatial outcome patterns generated and \fref{fig:univMoransIdesign} the resulting univariate Moran's I. The coordinates of the \textit{MAGPIE} alignments with jittered landmarks of the Vicari data are shown in \fref{fig:alignJitter}.

```{r design, fig.cap = "Spot grids for the parametric simulation study. The black squares are the locations of the spots of the first modality (X) and of the second (Y) for joint coordinate sets, the blue crosses the locations of the second modality (Y) for disjoint coordinate sets.\\label{fig:design}", fig.height = 4.5, fig.width = 4.5}
plotxy(xMat, yMat, pchY = "+", cex = 0.8)
```

```{r plotDesigns, fig.cap = "Visualisation of the different designs (rows) and outcomes (columns) for the parametric simulation study with disjoint coordinate sets.\\label{fig:designs}", fig.height = 10.5}
B <- 25^2
xB <- seq(0, 1, length.out = sqrt(B))
newGrid <- expand.grid(x = xB, y = xB)
# Ranges must be a lot shorter than dimensions of the image
designs <- Reduce(lapply(spatPatsShow, function(w) {
  dat <- makeXY(xMat, yMat,
    what = w, nSims = 2,
    nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
  )
  dat <- c(scaleZeroOne(dat$x[, 1]), scaleZeroOne(dat$y[, 1]))
  data.frame(
    Outcome = unlist(dat), variable = rep(c("X", "Y"), times = c(n, m)),
    pattern = w, rbind(xMat, yMat)
  )
}), f = rbind)
designs$pattern <- factor(designs$pattern,
  labels = spatPatsLabels,
  levels = spatPats, ordered = TRUE
)
ggplot(data = designs, aes(x = x, y = y, col = Outcome)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.1) +
  coord_fixed() +
  facet_grid(pattern ~ variable) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "yellow", high = "blue") +
  xlab("Coordinate 1") +
  ylab("Coordinate 2") +
  theme(strip.text.y = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90))
rm(designs)
```

```{r univMoransDesign, fig.cap = "Boxplots of univariate Moran's I values of the \\textit{x}-variable (y-axis) from the designs in \\fref{fig:designs} (x-axis), over 50 Monte-Carlo instances.\\label{fig:univMoransIdesign}", fig.height = 4, fig.width = 7.6}
wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(xMat, xMat, wo = "Gauss", eta = 1e-3)
diag(wm) <- 0
uniMorans <- lapply(spatPatsShow, function(w) {
  dat <- makeXY(xMat, yMat,
    what = w, nSims = 5e1,
    nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
  )
  x <- scale(dat$x)
  diag(crossprod(x, wm) %*% x)
})
names(uniMorans) <- factor(names(uniMorans), ordered = TRUE, labels = c("No spatial\npattern", "Independent\nGPs", "Dependent\nGPs", "Streak", "Zones", "Streak + GPs", "Zones + GPs"), levels = spatPatsShow)
boxplot(uniMorans, ylab = "Univariate Moran's I", xlas = .5, cex.axis = .65)
abline(h = 0, lty = "dotted")
rm(wm, uniMorans)
```

```{r coordPrep}
# Use the landmarks in S1_data, and continue the magpie pipeline from there to recover this alignment
if (!file.exists(magFile <- "Results/magpieCoords.RData")) {
  MagpieStxFiles <- list.files(vmcf <- "S1_data/VicariMagpieCoords/", pattern = "stx")
  sampleNamesMouse <- selfName(gsub("_stx.csv", "", MagpieStxFiles))
  magpieCoords <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sn) {
    msiCoords <- read.csv(paste0(vmcf, sn, "_msi.csv"), row.names = 1, header = FALSE)[, 1:3]
    colnames(msiCoords) <- c("in_tissue", "x", "y")
    msiCoords <- msiCoords[msiCoords$in_tissue == 1, ]
    visiumCoords <- read.csv(paste0(vmcf, sn, "_stx.csv"), header = FALSE, col.names = c("barcode", "in_tissue", "array_row", "array_col", "x", "y"))
    visiumCoords <- visiumCoords[visiumCoords$in_tissue == 1, ]
    rownames(visiumCoords) <- visiumCoords$barcode
    list(
      "Stx" = as.matrix(visiumCoords[rownames(exprObjs[[sn]]), c("x", "y")]),
      "Msi" = as.matrix(msiCoords[, c("x", "y")])
    )
  })
  # Msi coordinates have been transformed
  # Turn them flat, and shift to origin for plotting
  magpieCoords <- lapply(magpieCoords, function(x) {
    x <- rotate_clouds(x)
    Mins <- apply(vapply(x, FUN.VALUE = double(2), FUN = function(y) {
      apply(y, 2, min)
    }), 1, min)
    lapply(x, function(y) {
      y[, "x"] <- (y[, "x"] - Mins[1])
      y[, "y"] <- (y[, "y"] - Mins[2])
      y
    })
  })
  save(magpieCoords, file = magFile)
} else {
  load(magFile)
}
```

```{r mainPaperReadIn}
# Metabolomics
if (!file.exists(msiVicariFile <- "Data/msiVicari.RData")) {
  # Download msi libraries
  downloadAndUnzip("https://data.mendeley.com/public-files/datasets/w7nw4km7xd/files/1ed125c4-e082-429b-bdfd-e59a2aa856b1/file_downloaded", destfile = MsiFold <- "Data/Vicari2024/Msi", method = "wget")
  msiFiles <- list.files(MsiFold, pattern = "smamsi.csv", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  msiObjs0 <- lapply(msiFiles, function(x) as.matrix(read.csv(x)))
  # Correct naming mistake, confirmed by M. Vicari
  names(msiObjs0) <- gsub("V11T17-085", "V11T16-085", sapply(gsub("_smamsi.csv", "", msiFiles), function(x) {
    tmp <- gregexpr("/", x)[[1]]
    substr(x, tmp[length(tmp)] + 1, tmp[length(tmp)] + 13)
  }))
  msiObjs <- lapply(msiObjs0, function(x) {
    as.matrix(x[, !(colnames(x) %in% c("x", "y"))])
  })
  locObjsMsi <- lapply(msiObjs0, function(x) {
    as.matrix(x[, c("x", "y")])
  })
  # Remove empty samples
  idMSI <- lapply(msiObjs, function(x) {
    which(rowSums(x[, -c(1, 2)]) > 0)
  })
  msiObjs <- mapply(msiObjs, idMSI, FUN = function(x, id) {
    x[id, ]
  }, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
  locObjsMsi <- mapply(locObjsMsi, idMSI, FUN = function(x, id) {
    x[id, ]
  }, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
  for (i in sampleNamesMouse) { # Take pixel names from the alignment
    rownames(msiObjs[[i]]) <- rownames(locObjsMsi[[i]]) <- rownames(magpieCoords[[i]]$Msi)
  }
  # Download supplementary data
  download.file("https://static-content.springer.com/esm/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41587-023-01937-y/MediaObjects/41587_2023_1937_MOESM2_ESM.xlsx", destfile = supFileVic <- "Data/Vicari2024/41587_2023_1937_MOESM2_ESM.xlsx", method = "curl", extra = "-L -O")
  metaboliteInfo <- read.xlsx(supFileVic, sheet = 3, startRow = 3, rows = c(3:18, 25, 26))
  unMets <- unique(unlist(lapply(msiObjs, colnames)))
  names(unMets) <- unMets
  unMetsIdentified <- sapply(as.numeric(metaboliteInfo$`m/z`), function(x) {
    unMets[which.min(abs(as.numeric(gsub("X", "", unMets)) - x))]
  })
  unMetsIdentified <- unMetsIdentified[idMet <- sapply(as.numeric(metaboliteInfo$`m/z`), function(x) {
    min(abs(as.numeric(gsub("X", "", unMets)) - x))
  }) < 0.1] # remove bad matches
  names(unMetsIdentified) <- make.names(metaboliteInfo$Name)[idMet]
  unMetsIdentified <- unMetsIdentified[order(as.numeric(gsub("X", "", unMetsIdentified)))]
  names(unMetsIdentified) <- gsub("\\.+", ".", sub("\\.$", "", names(unMetsIdentified)))
  names(unMetsIdentified)[names(unMetsIdentified) == "Dopamine.double.derivatized"] <- "Dopamine"
  names(unMetsIdentified)[names(unMetsIdentified) == "X3.4.Dihydroxyphenylacetic.acid.DOPAC.double.derivatized"] <- "X3.4.Dihydroxyphenylacetic.acid.DOPAC"
  save(msiObjs, locObjsMsi, unMetsIdentified, file = msiVicariFile)
} else {
  load(msiVicariFile)
}
# Spatial Tx
if (!file.exists(stxVicariFile <- "Data/stxVicari.RData")) {
  # Figshare article ID (from the URL)
  article_id <- 22778920
  base_url <- paste0("https://api.figshare.com/v2/articles/", article_id, "/files")
  StxFolder <- "Data/Vicari2024/Stx"
  page <- 1
  all_files <- list()
  repeat {
    url <- paste0(base_url, "?page=", page, "&page_size=", 100)
    res <- GET(url)
    files <- fromJSON(content(res, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
    if (length(files) == 0) break
    all_files[[page]] <- files
    page <- page + 1
  }
  files_df <- base::do.call(rbind, all_files)
  # Download location files
  # Filter tissue_positions_list.csv
  target_files <- files_df[grep("tissue_positions_list\\.csv$", files_df$name), ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(target_files))) {
    file_url <- target_files$download_url[i]
    file_name <- target_files$name[i]
    GET(file_url, write_disk(file.path(StxFolder, file_name), overwrite = TRUE))
  }
  stxFiles <- list.files(StxFolder, pattern = "tissue_positions_list.csv", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  locObjsStx <- lapply(stxFiles, read.csv, header = FALSE, row.names = 1)
  # Locations
  locObjsStx <- lapply(locObjsStx, function(x) {
    colnames(x) <- c("underTissue", "x", "y", "un1", "un2")
    as.matrix(x[x[, "underTissue"] == 1, c("x", "y")]) # Subset for spots under tissue
  })
  names(locObjsStx) <- sapply(sub("RNA_", "", stxFiles), function(x) {
    tmp <- gregexpr("_tissue_positions_list.csv", x)[[1]]
    substr(x, 21, tmp[1] - 1)
  })
  # Download expression libraries
  exprFiles <- files_df[grep("RNA_filtered_feature_bc_matrix\\.h5$", files_df$name), ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(exprFiles))) {
    file_url <- exprFiles$download_url[i]
    file_name <- exprFiles$name[i]
    GET(file_url, write_disk(file.path(StxFolder, file_name), overwrite = TRUE))
  }
  exprFiles <- list.files(StxFolder, pattern = "filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  exprObjs <- lapply(exprFiles, h5read, name = "matrix")
  names(exprObjs) <- sapply(exprFiles, function(x) {
    tmp <- gregexpr("_RNA_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5", x)[[1]]
    substr(x, 21, tmp[1] - 1)
  })
  minLib <- 1e3
  exprObjs <- lapply(exprObjs, function(y) {
    x <- t(Matrix::sparseMatrix(
      i = y[["indices"]], p = y[["indptr"]], x = y[["data"]],
      dims = y[["shape"]], index1 = FALSE, dimnames = list(y$features$name, y$barcodes)
    ))
    # Based on dior::h5_to_matrix, with updated name slots
    colnames(x) <- make.names(colnames(x)) # Enforce valid names
    tmp <- x[rowSums(x) >= minLib, colSums(x) > 0]
    tmp[, -which(duplicated(colnames(tmp)))]
  })
  for (i in names(exprObjs)) {
    locObjsStx[[i]] <- locObjsStx[[i]][rownames(exprObjs[[i]]), ]
  }
  # Metadata
  download.file("https://data.mendeley.com/public-files/datasets/w7nw4km7xd/files/abed16be-70ce-4991-a0df-85b475d7aa44/file_downloaded", destfile = "Data/Vicari2024/metadata.csv")
  metaVicari0 <- read.csv("Data/Vicari2024/metadata.csv")
  metaVicari <- metaVicari0[metaVicari0$Sample.ID %in% names(exprObjs), c("Data.Type", "Sample.ID", "Matrix", "sample", "Condition", "Brain.area", "Visium.Protocol")]
  save(exprObjs, metaVicari, locObjsStx, file = stxVicariFile)
} else {
  load(stxVicariFile)
}
sampleNames <- selfName(intersect(names(msiObjs), names(exprObjs)))
sampleNamesMouse <- grep(value = TRUE, "V11T17-102", invert = TRUE, sampleNames)
indivMouse <- sapply(sampleNamesMouse, substr, 1, 10)
rm(locObjsMsi, locObjsStx)
```

```{r jitterLandMarks}
# To be found in S1_data
landmarksDf <- read.csv("S1_data/landmarks.csv", row.names = 1)
# Landmarks are given here, the rest of the MAGPIE pipeline is available from https://core-bioinformatics.github.io/magpie/tutorial/SMA_tutorial.html, using the no_HE option and 'affine transformation'
nSimsJitter <- 10 # 20
sdJitter <- c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3)
landmarksGrid <- expand.grid("rep" = seq_len(nSimsJitter), "sdJitter" = sdJitter, "sample" = sampleNamesMouse, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
lmFolder <- "Results/landmarks/"
distorted <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(landmarksGrid)), function(i) {
  sam <- landmarksGrid[i, "sample"]
  rep <- landmarksGrid[i, "rep"]
  sd <- landmarksGrid[i, "sdJitter"]
  lm <- landmarksDf[landmarksDf$sample == sam, c("X_left", "Y_left", "X_left", "X_right")]
  if (!file.exists(File <- paste0(lmFolder, "rep", rep, "sd", sd, "sample", sam, ".csv"))) {
    lm$X_left <- lm$X_left + rnorm(length(lm$X_left), sd = sd)
    lm$Y_left <- lm$Y_left + rnorm(length(lm$Y_left), sd = sd)
    write.csv(lm, file = File, row.names = FALSE)
  } # Jitter landmarks and store for alignment with magpie
})
rm(distorted, landmarksDf, landmarksGrid)
foo <- gc()
```

```{r plotExploreJitter, fig.cap = "Real alignments (top plots) and examples of alignment after jittering landmarks with different standard deviations (SD) (other plots) of the first section of the three mice in the Vicari data (plot titles). Metabolic spots are black and transcriptomic spots are blue.\\label{fig:alignJitter}", fig.height = 9.5}
par(mfcol = c(4, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, .6))
foo <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse[c(1, 4, 7)], function(sam) {
  visiumCoords <- read.csv(header = FALSE, paste0("Data/Vicari2024/Vicari2024_Magpie/input/", sam, "/visium/spatial/tissue_positions_list.csv"), col.names = c("barcode", "in_tissue", "array_row", "array_col", "x", "y"))
  visiumCoords <- visiumCoords[visiumCoords$in_tissue == 1, ]
  msiCoords <- read.csv(paste0(
    "Data/Vicari2024/Vicari2024_Magpie/", sam,
    "/spaceranger/spatial/tissue_positions_list.csv"
  ), row.names = 1, header = FALSE)[, 1:3]
  colnames(msiCoords) <- c("in_tissue", "x", "y")
  msiCoords <- msiCoords[msiCoords$in_tissue == 1, ]
  rc <- rotate_clouds(list(visiumCoords[, c("x", "y")], msiCoords[, c("x", "y")]))
  plotxy(rc$Msi, rc$Stx, main = paste0("Real alignment\n", sam), cex = 0.1, cex.main = .85)
  lapply(sdJitter[c(3, 5, 6)], function(sd) {
    dat <- read.csv(paste0(lmFolder, "newCoords_rep1sd", sd, "sample", sam, ".csv"), header = FALSE)[, c(3, 4)]
    rc <- rotate_clouds(list(visiumCoords[, c("x", "y")], dat))
    plotxy(rc$Msi, rc$Stx, main = paste0("Jittter SD=", sd, "\n", sam), cex = 0.1, cex.main = .85)
  })
})
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
rm(foo)
```

\clearpage

## Results

### Nonparametric simulations

#### Single-image analysis

\fref{fig:nonParAll} in the main text summarizes the results of the nonparametric simulations on single images, here we show more exhaustive results. Figs \ref{fig:qqGodfrey}-\ref{fig:qqVicari} demonstrate that bivariate Moran's I with random shift or randomisation null distribution, and GAMs when both modalities are permuted, often have p-value distributions that are larger than uniform, whereas many existing methods have smaller-than-uniform p-value distributions. Next we investigate which features are prone to incur false findings. As measure of SAC, we calculate univariate Moran's I values $\mb X^t \mb W \mb X$ for all features of the original data, constructing the weight matrix as for the bivariate case, with Gaussian weights and decay parameter $\eta = 10^{-3}$, and setting the diagonal to zero. Boxplots of the univariate Moran's I values thus calculated are shown in Figs \ref{fig:boxplotMoransIgodfrey}-\ref{fig:boxplotMoransIvicari}, showing considerable SAC in almost all images, with the metabolome exhibiting the strongest SAC. We model the probability that a feature is detected as significantly associated with a permuted feature from the other modality through generalized linear mixed models using the *glmer* function in the *lme4* package \parencite{Bates2015} with a binomial outcome distribution. Fixed effects were the dataset, the non-permuted modality, the interaction of the previous two and univariate Moran's I of the non-permuted feature; random intercepts were included for the sections. Separate models were fitted per method. Modeled probabilities of detection based in the fixed effects are plotted in \fref{fig:moransIuniFindings}, revealing that for the methods found liberal in the simulations, features with high SAC in the non-permuted modality are more likely to incur false findings.

```{r loadGodfrey}
if (!file.exists(godfreyFile <- "Data/godfrey2025.RData")) {
  # Metadata on files
  meta_url <- paste0("https://dataverse.harvard.edu/api/datasets/:persistentId/?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/GZFCWC")
  meta <- fromJSON(content(GET(meta_url), as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
  files <- meta$data$latestVersion$files
  # Extract file names and file IDs
  file_df <- data.frame(
    name = files$dataFile$filename,
    id = files$dataFile$id,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  # Select and download h5ad-files
  targets <- file_df[grepl("\\.h5ad$", file_df$name), ]
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(targets))) {
    file_id <- targets$id[i]
    fname <- paste0("Data/Godfrey2025/", targets$name[i])
    url <- paste0("https://dataverse.harvard.edu/api/access/datafile/", file_id)
    download.file(url, destfile = fname, method = "curl", extra = "-L -O")
  }
  # Read in h5ad files
  godfreyFiles <- list.files("Data/Godfrey2025", full.names = TRUE, pattern = "\\.h5ad$")
  godfrey <- lapply(godfreyFiles, function(f) {
    x <- read_h5ad(f)
    Stx <- as.matrix(x$layers[["raw"]])
    colnames(Stx) <- make.names(colnames(Stx))
    Coords <- as.matrix(x$obsm[["spatial"]])
    colnames(Coords) <- c("x", "y")
    Coords[, "y"] <- max(Coords[, "y"]) - Coords[, "y"] # Make orientation match paper
    Met <- as.matrix(x$uns[["msi"]])
    Annot <- x$obs$annotation # Pathologists' annotation
    colnames(Met) <- make.names(x$uns[["mz_features"]])
    rownames(Met) <- rownames(Coords) <- rownames(Stx)
    list(Coord = Coords, Stx = Stx, Msi = Met, Annot = Annot)
  })
  names(godfrey) <- gsub(".h5ad", "", list.files("Data/Godfrey2025", pattern = "\\.h5ad$"))
  # Download supplementary material xlsx-file
  download.file("http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/action/downloadSupplement?doi=10.1002%2Fanie.202502028&file=anie202502028-sup-0002-TABLES.xlsx", destfile = "Data/Godfrey2025/anie202502028-sup-0002-tables.xlsx", method = "curl", extra = "-L -O")
  save(godfrey, file = godfreyFile)
}
numFeatsGodfrey <- 3e1
if (!file.exists(godfreyIdFile <- "Data/godfrey2025identified.RData")) {
  load(godfreyFile)
  # Read in xlsx, first sheet on MIS annotations
  godfreyMetTable1 <- read.xlsx("Data/Godfrey2025/anie202502028-sup-0002-tables.xlsx", sheet = 1)
  allMets <- make.names(unique(godfreyMetTable1$`Observed.m/z`))
  godfreyIdentified <- lapply(godfrey, function(x) {
    x$Msi <- x$Msi[, sapply(allMets, grep, colnames(x$Msi))]
    x$Stx <- x$Stx[, colSums(x$Stx) > 0]
    x
  }) # Subset to identified m/z
  save(godfreyIdentified, allMets, file = godfreyIdFile)
} else {
  load(godfreyIdFile)
}
samplesGodfrey <- selfName(names(godfreyIdentified))
godfreyMetTable <- read.xlsx("Data/Godfrey2025/anie202502028-sup-0002-tables.xlsx", sheet = 2)[
  ,
  c("Sample", "Metabolite/Lipid", "Observed.MZ", "Genes")
]
# Read xlsx on individual correlations
mappingSamples <- samplesGodfrey
names(mappingSamples) <- c("BC-1.2", "BC-1", "BC-2", "BC-3", "LC-1", "LC-2", "LC-3")
godfreyMetTable$MZmatched <- sapply(paste0("X", godfreyMetTable$Observed.MZ),
  grep,
  x = allMets, value = TRUE
) # Match m/z's
allMetsCommon <- godfreyMetTable$`Metabolite/Lipid`[match(allMets, godfreyMetTable$MZmatched)]
names(allMetsCommon) <- allMets
nScramsGodfrey <- 2e1
godfreyGrid <- expand.grid(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "what" = c("Stx", "Met", "Stx_Met"),
  "sample" = samplesGodfrey, "rep" = seq_len(nScramsGodfrey)
)
```

```{r saveVicariSbivar, eval = FALSE}
# Save Vicari data for in sbivar package
numSbivar <- 5
sampleNamesMouseSbivar <- sampleNamesMouse[-(1:3)]
selGenesVicariSbivar <- selfName(filterFeatsList(lapply(exprObjs[sampleNamesMouseSbivar], as.matrix), numFeats = numSbivar, allPresent = FALSE))
selGenesVicariSbivar <- c("Fth1", "mt.Atp6", "Sparc", "Gnas", "Pcp4")
selMetsVicariSbivar <- unMetsIdentified[match(c("Dopamine", "Tocopherol", "Taurine", "Histidine", "GABA"), names(unMetsIdentified))]
TranscriptCoords <- lapply(magpieCoords[sampleNamesMouseSbivar], function(x) x$Stx)
MetaboliteCoords <- lapply(magpieCoords[sampleNamesMouseSbivar], function(x) x$Msi)
TranscriptOutcomes <- lapply(exprObjs[sampleNamesMouseSbivar], function(x) as.matrix(x[, selGenesVicariSbivar]))
MetaboliteOutcomes <- lapply(msiObjs[sampleNamesMouseSbivar], function(x) {
  mat <- x[, intersect(colnames(x), selMetsVicariSbivar)]
  colnames(mat) <- names(unMetsIdentified)[match(colnames(mat), unMetsIdentified)]
  mat
})
Vicari <- list(
  "TranscriptCoords" = TranscriptCoords, "TranscriptOutcomes" = TranscriptOutcomes,
  "MetaboliteCoords" = MetaboliteCoords, "MetaboliteOutcomes" = MetaboliteOutcomes
)
save(Vicari, file = "sbivar/data/Vicari.RData")
# tools::resaveRdaFiles("data") #To be run in package
```

```{r godfreysim}
etaSim <- 2e-4
if (!file.exists(godfreyScrambleFile <- "Results/godfreyScramble.RData")) {
  load(godfreyFile)
  godfreyFiltered <- lapply(godfrey, function(x) {
    x$Stx <- filterFeats(x$Stx, numFeats = numFeatsGodfrey)
    x$Msi <- filterFeats(x$Msi, numFeats = numFeatsGodfrey)
    x
  })
  godfreyScramble <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(godfreyGrid)), function(ii) {
    what <- godfreyGrid[ii, "what"]
    sample <- godfreyGrid[ii, "sample"]
    rep <- godfreyGrid[ii, "rep"]
    dat <- godfreyFiltered[[sample]]
    if (grepl("Stx", what)) {
      dat$Stx <- dat$Stx[sample(nrow(dat$Stx)), ]
    }
    if (grepl("Met", what)) {
      dat$Msi <- dat$Msi[sample(nrow(dat$Msi)), ]
    }
    featsGrid <- expand.grid(stringsAsFactors = FALSE, gene = colnames(dat$Stx), met = colnames(dat$Msi))
    distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(dat$Coord))
    rownames(dat$Stx) <- rownames(dat$Msi) <- rownames(dat$Coord)
    X <- normMat(dat$Stx, "rel")
    Y <- normMat(dat$Msi, "rel")
    samNames <- intersect(rownames(X), rownames(Y))
    dat$Coord <- dat$Coord[samNames, ]
    X <- X[samNames, ]
    Y <- Y[samNames, ]
    dat$Coord <- sbivar:::moveCoords(dat$Coord)
    dat$Coord <- dat$Coord / max(dat$Coord)
    wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(dat$Coord, dat$Coord, wo = "Gauss", eta = etaSim)
    wm[wm < 1e-30] <- 0
    listWgeary <- listw2U(mat2listw(wm, style = "W"))
    dgr <- as.matrix(spatialreg::as_dgRMatrix_listw(listWgeary))
    wc <- spweights.constants(listWgeary, zero.policy = FALSE, adjust.n = TRUE)
    listW <- mat2listw(wm, style = "M")
    prepCzaplewski <- prepareCzaplewskiWeights(wm)
    H <- diag(n) - 1 / n
    # Moran's I
    Ixy <- crossprod(X, wm) %*% Y
    ## SpatialDM
    StxScaled <- scale(X, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    MetScaled <- scale(Y, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    IxyScaled <- crossprod(StxScaled, wm) %*% MetScaled
    nswm <- n / sum(wm)
    IxyvarSpatialDM <- (n / (n - 1))^2 * 1 / sum(wm)^2 * tr((H %*% wm %*% H)^2)
    ## Czaplewski
    pValsLoop <- vapply(seq_len(nrow(featsGrid)), FUN.VALUE = double(9), function(i) {
      x <- X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]]
      y <- Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]]
      resCza <- getVarCzaplewski(x, y, prepCzaplewski)
      Ixynorm <- nswm * IxyScaled[featsGrid[i, "gene"], featsGrid[i, "met"]] /
        sqrt(sum(MetScaled[, featsGrid[i, "met"]]^2) * sum(StxScaled[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]]^2))
      out <- c(
        "MoransI_Czaplewski" = makePval((Ixy[featsGrid[i, "gene"], featsGrid[i, "met"]]
        - resCza["EIxy"]) / sqrt(resCza["varIxy"])),
        "MoransI_SpatialDM" = makePval(Ixynorm / sqrt(IxyvarSpatialDM)),
        "SpaGene" = SpaGene_LR(
          expr = rbind("x" = dat$Stx[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], "y" = dat$Msi[, featsGrid[i, "met"]]), perm = nPerms,
          location = dat$Coord, LRpair = data.frame("x" = "x", "y" = "y")
        )[["pval"]],
        # This SpaGene test on raw data, normalisation happens inside function
        "LeesL_asymptotic" = lee.test(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]], listW)$p.value,
        "LeesL_permutation" = lee.mc(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]], listW, nsim = nPerms)$p.value,
        "GearysC_asymptotic" = gearybi.test(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]],
          listWgeary,
          randomisation = FALSE
        )$p.value,
        "GearysC_randomisation" = gearybi.test(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]],
          listWgeary,
          randomisation = TRUE
        )$p.value,
        "PearsonCor" = cor.test(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]])$p.value,
        "modTtest" = modified.ttest(X[, featsGrid[i, "gene"]], Y[, featsGrid[i, "met"]], dat$Coord)$p.value
      )
      return(out)
    })
    scramInst <- cbind(t(pValsLoop), "MoransI_permutation" = c(IxyPermPvals))
    sample <- godfreyGrid[ii, "sample"]
    rownames(scramInst) <- sbivar:::makeNames(colnames(godfreyFiltered[[sample]]$Stx), colnames(godfreyFiltered[[sample]]$Msi))
    scramInst
  })
  save(godfreyScramble, file = godfreyScrambleFile)
} else {
  load(godfreyScrambleFile)
}
```

```{r gamsScrambleGodfrey}
if (!file.exists(realFitScrambleGodfreyFile <- "Results/realFitsScrambleGodfrey.RData")) {
  load(godfreyFile)
  godfreyFiltered <- lapply(godfrey, function(x) {
    x$Stx <- filterFeats(x$Stx, numFeats = numFeatsGodfrey)
    x$Msi <- filterFeats(x$Msi, numFeats = numFeatsGodfrey)
    x
  })
  realFitsScrambleGodfrey <- mclapply(seq_len(nrow(godfreyGrid)), mc.cores = nCores, function(ii) {
    what <- godfreyGrid[ii, "what"]
    sn <- godfreyGrid[ii, "sample"]
    rep <- godfreyGrid[ii, "rep"]
    dat <- godfreyFiltered[[sn]]
    if (grepl("Stx", what)) {
      dat$Stx <- dat$Stx[sample(nrow(dat$Stx)), ]
    }
    if (grepl("Met", what)) {
      dat$Msi <- dat$Msi[sample(nrow(dat$Msi)), ]
    }
    rownames(dat$Stx) <- rownames(dat$Msi) <- rownames(dat$Coord)
    Moranres <- sbivar(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord,
      method = "Moran", normX = "rel",
      normY = "rel", verbose = FALSE
    )$result
    GAMres <- sbivar(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord,
      method = "GAM",
      families = FamiliesGodfrey, verbose = FALSE
    )$result
    ModTres <- sbivar(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord,
      method = "Modified",
      normX = "rel", normY = "rel", verbose = FALSE
    )$result
    MoranResPerm <- MoransIpermutation(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord, dat$Coord,
      nPerms = nPerms,
      normX = "rel", normY = "rel"
    )
    CorRes <- corTests(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, normX = "rel", normY = "rel")[, "pVal"]
    CCresMoran <- CCtestEtas(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord, dat$Coord, nPerms, normX = "rel", normY = "rel")
    list(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = Moranres, "GAM" = GAMres, "modTtest" = ModTres,
      "PearsonCor" = CorRes, "MoransI_permutation" = MoranResPerm,
      "MoransI_RandomShift" = CCresMoran
    )
  })
  save(realFitsScrambleGodfrey, file = realFitScrambleGodfreyFile)
}
if (!file.exists(realFitScrambleGodfreyFilePrep <- "Results/realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep.RData")) {
  load(realFitScrambleGodfreyFile)
  realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep <- lapply(realFitsScrambleGodfrey, function(x) {
    x[c("MoransI_sbivar", "GAM")] <- lapply(x[c("MoransI_sbivar", "GAM")], function(xx) {
      xx[, "pVal"]
    })
    x[c("MoransI_permutation", "MoransI_RandomShift")] <- lapply(x[c("MoransI_permutation", "MoransI_RandomShift")], function(yy) {
      out <- c(yy)
      names(out) <- sbivar:::makeNames(rownames(yy), colnames(yy))
      out
    })
    lapply(x[c("MoransI_sbivar", "GAM", "MoransI_permutation", "MoransI_RandomShift")], function(yy) {
      data.frame("value" = yy, "featurePair" = names(yy))
    })
  })
  save(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep, file = realFitScrambleGodfreyFilePrep)
} else {
  load(realFitScrambleGodfreyFilePrep)
}
```

```{r univariateMoransIGodfrey}
if (!file.exists(realMoranGodfreyUniFile <- "Results/realMoranUniGodfrey.RData")) {
  realFitsMoranUniGodfrey <- lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sn) {
    x <- scale(normMat(godfreyFiltered[[sn]]$Stx, "rel"))
    y <- scale(normMat(godfreyFiltered[[sn]]$Msi, "rel"))
    Coord <- sbivar:::moveCoords(godfreyFiltered[[sn]]$Coord)
    Coord <- Coord / max(Coord)
    wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(Coord, Coord, wo = "Gauss", eta = 1e-3)
    diag(wm) <- 0
    dimnames(wm) <- list(rownames(Coord), rownames(Coord))
    IxyStx <- crossprod(x, wm[rownames(x), rownames(x)]) %*% x
    IxyMsi <- crossprod(y, wm) %*% y
    list("IxyStx" = IxyStx, "IxyMsi" = IxyMsi, "maxI" = svd(wm, nu = 0, nv = 0)$d[1] * (nrow(wm) - 1) * (ncol(wm) - 1))
  })
  save(realFitsMoranUniGodfrey, file = realMoranGodfreyUniFile)
} else {
  load(realMoranGodfreyUniFile)
}
```

```{r univariateMoransI}
if (!file.exists(realMoranUniFile <- "Results/realMoranUniVicari.RData")) {
  VicariFilteredScramble <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(x) {
    Stx <- as.matrix(filterFeats(exprObjs[[x]], numFeats = numFeatsVicariScramble))
    Msi <- filterFeats(msiObjs[[x]], numFeats = numFeatsVicariScramble)
    rownames(Msi) <- rownames(magpieCoords[[x]]$Msi)
    list("Stx" = Stx, "Msi" = Msi)
  })
  realFitsMoranUniVicari <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sn) {
    x <- scale(normMat(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Stx, "rel"))
    y <- scale(normMat(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Msi, "rel"))
    Coords <- sbivar:::moveTwoCoords(magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx, magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi)
    wmStx <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(Coords$Cx, Coords$Cx, wo = "Gauss", eta = 1e-3)
    dimnames(wmStx) <- list(rownames(Coords$Cx), rownames(Coords$Cx))
    IxyStx <- crossprod(x, wmStx[rownames(x), rownames(x)]) %*% x
    wmMsi <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(Coords$Ey, Coords$Ey, wo = "Gauss", eta = 1e-3)
    dimnames(wmMsi) <- list(rownames(Coords$Ey), rownames(Coords$Ey))
    IxyMsi <- crossprod(y, wmMsi) %*% y
    list(
      "IxyStx" = IxyStx, "IxyMsi" = IxyMsi,
      "maxIStx" = svd(wmStx, nu = 0, nv = 0)$d[1] * (nrow(wmStx) - 1) * (ncol(wmStx) - 1),
      "maxIMsi" = svd(wmMsi, nu = 0, nv = 0)$d[1] * (nrow(wmMsi) - 1) * (ncol(wmMsi) - 1)
    )
  })
  save(realFitsMoranUniVicari, file = realMoranUniFile)
} else {
  load(realMoranUniFile)
}
```

```{r processScrambleGodfrey, fig.cap = "Type I error (y-axis) for different methods (colour) when permuting observations of one or two modalities (rows) of the sections of the Godfrey data (colums). The dotted horizontal line indicates the significance level of 0.05.\\label{fig:nonParResGodfrey}"}
moltRealGodfrey <- melt(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep, id.vars = "featurePair")
colnames(moltRealGodfrey)[4:5] <- c("Method", "rep")
moltRealGodfrey <- cbind(moltRealGodfrey, godfreyGrid[moltRealGodfrey[, "rep"], ])
moltRealGodfrey$what <- factor(moltRealGodfrey$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealGodfrey$Method <- factor(moltRealGodfrey$Method,
  levels = methLevels,
  labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealGodfreyAgg <- aggregate(data = moltRealGodfrey, x = value ~ Method + sample + what, FUN = getPower)
moltRealGodfreyAgg$Dataset <- "Godfrey"
realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepAdj <- sapply(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep, function(x) {
  sapply(x, function(yy) {
    any(p.adjust(yy$value, "BH") < sigLevel)
  })
})
scrambleGodfreyFdr <- apply(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepAdj, 1, function(x) tapply(x, godfreyGrid$what, mean))
godfreyScrambleExistPrep <- lapply(godfreyScramble, function(x) {
  lapply(selfName(colnames(x)), function(y) {
    data.frame("value" = x[, y], "featurePair" = rownames(x))
  })
})
realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepBoth <- mapply(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep, godfreyScrambleExistPrep,
  FUN = function(x, y) {
    c(x, y)
  }, SIMPLIFY = FALSE
)
moltRealGodfreyExist <- melt(godfreyScramble)
colnames(moltRealGodfreyExist) <- c("featurePair", "Method", "value", "rep")
moltRealGodfreyExist <- cbind(moltRealGodfreyExist, godfreyGrid[moltRealGodfreyExist[, "rep"], ])
moltRealGodfreyExist$what <- factor(moltRealGodfreyExist$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealGodfreyExist$Method <- factor(moltRealGodfreyExist$Method,
  levels = methLevels,
  labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealGodfreyExistAgg <- aggregate(
  data = moltRealGodfreyExist,
  x = value ~ Method + sample + what, FUN = getPower
)
moltRealGodfreyExistAgg$Dataset <- "Godfrey"
moltRealGodfreyExistAggAll <- rbind(moltRealGodfreyAgg, moltRealGodfreyExistAgg)
moransIincurringGodfrey <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(scrambleVec[1:2], function(what) {
  morName <- switch(what,
    "Stx" = "IxyMsi",
    "Met" = "IxyStx"
  ) # Take non-permuted modality
  tmp <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(id <- which(godfreyGrid$what == what), function(i) {
    Sam <- godfreyGrid[i, "sample"]
    uniMoran <- diag(realFitsMoranUniGodfrey[[Sam]][[morName]])
    names(uniMoran) <- make.names(names(uniMoran))
    base::do.call(rbind, lapply(selfName(names(realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepBoth[[i]])), function(nn) {
      yy <- realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepBoth[[i]][[nn]]
      featNames <- sapply(strsplit(yy$featurePair, split = "__"), function(x) {
        x[switch(what,
          "Stx" = 2,
          "Met" = 1
        )]
      })
      boo <- tapply(yy$value, featNames, function(x) sum(x < sigLevel, na.rm = TRUE))
      data.frame("findings" = boo, "id" = i, "MoransI" = uniMoran[names(boo)], "Method" = nn)
    }))
  }))
  tmp2 <- aggregate(findings ~ MoransI + id + Method, data = tmp, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  cbind(tmp2, godfreyGrid[tmp2$id, ])
}))
moransIincurringGodfrey$what <- factor(moransIincurringGodfrey$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moransIincurringGodfrey$negFindings <- numFeatsGodfrey - moransIincurringGodfrey$findings
godfreyNum <- nrow(moltRealGodfrey) + nrow(moltRealGodfreyExist)
subSamQQ <- 15
tmpDat <- rbind(moltRealGodfrey[, -2], moltRealGodfreyExist)[sample(godfreyNum, godfreyNum / subSamQQ), 1:6]
rm(
  godfreyFiltered, realFitsScrambleGodfrey, moltRealGodfreyExistAgg, moltRealGodfreyExist, moltRealGodfrey, realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepBoth,
  realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrepAdj, godfreyScramble, godfreyScrambleExistPrep
)
```

```{r vicariScramble}
nScramblesVicari <- 20
numFeatsVicariScramble <- 3e1
vicariGrid <- expand.grid(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "what" = c("Stx", "Met", "Stx_Met"),
  "sample" = sampleNamesMouse, "rep" = seq_len(nScramblesVicari)
)
if (!file.exists(realFitScrambleFile <- "Results/realFitsScrambleVicari.RData")) {
  VicariFilteredScramble <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(x) {
    Stx <- as.matrix(filterFeats(exprObjs[[x]], numFeats = numFeatsVicariScramble))
    Msi <- filterFeats(msiObjs[[x]], numFeats = numFeatsVicariScramble)
    rownames(Msi) <- rownames(magpieCoords[[x]]$Msi)
    list("Stx" = Stx, "Msi" = Msi)
  })
  realFitsScrambleVicari <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(vicariGrid)), function(ii) {
    what <- vicariGrid[ii, "what"]
    sn <- vicariGrid[ii, "sample"]
    rep <- vicariGrid[ii, "rep"]
    xMat <- magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx
    yMat <- magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi
    if (grepl("Stx", what)) {
      xMat <- xMat[sample(nrow(xMat)), ]
    }
    if (grepl("Met", what)) {
      yMat <- yMat[sample(nrow(yMat)), ]
    }
    Moranres <- sbivar(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Stx, VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Msi, xMat, yMat,
      method = "Moran", normX = "rel", normY = "rel"
    )
    GAMres <- sbivar(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Stx, VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Msi, xMat, yMat,
      families = FamiliesVicari, method = "GAM"
    )
    MoranResPerm <- MoransIpermutation(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Stx, VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Msi, xMat, yMat, nPerms = nPermsScramble, normX = "rel", normY = "rel")
    MoranRandomShift <- CCtestEtas(VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Stx, VicariFilteredScramble[[sn]]$Msi, magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx, magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi, nPerms = nPermsScramble)
    scrambleResVicari <- list(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = Moranres, "GAM" = GAMres,
      "MoranResPerm" = MoranResPerm, "MoranRandomShift" = MoranRandomShift
    )
    scrambleResVicari
  })
  save(realFitsScrambleVicari, file = realFitScrambleFile)
} else {
  load(realFitScrambleFile)
}
realFitsScrambleVicariPrep <- lapply(realFitsScrambleVicari, function(x) {
  nams <- makeRowNames(x$GAM$result)
  x$GAM$result <- as.matrix(x$GAM$result[, 3:6])
  rownames(x$GAM$result) <- nams
  x[c("MoransI_sbivar", "GAM")] <- lapply(x[c("MoransI_sbivar", "GAM")], function(xx) {
    xx$result[, "pVal"]
  })
  x[c("MoranResPerm", "MoranRandomShift")] <- lapply(x[c("MoranResPerm", "MoranRandomShift")], function(yy) {
    out <- c(yy)
    names(out) <- sbivar:::makeNames(rownames(yy), colnames(yy))
    out
  })
  names(x)[3:4] <- c("MoransI_permutation", "MoransI_RandomShift")
  lapply(x, function(yy) {
    data.frame("value" = yy, "featurePair" = names(yy))
  })
})
rm(realFitsScrambleVicari)
```

```{r qqplotsGodfrey, fig.cap = "QQ-plots of observed p-values (y-axis) versus standard uniform quantiles (x-axis) for nonparametric simulations based on the Godfrey data, for different methods (colour) when permuting observations of one or two modalities (columns) of the sections (rows). The solid black line has slope 1 and intercept 0.\\label{fig:qqGodfrey}", fig.height=9.5}
ggplot(data = tmpDat, aes(sample = value, col = Method)) +
  geom_qq(distribution = stats::qunif, size = 0.25) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(what), rows = vars(sample)) +
  ylab("Observed quantiles") +
  xlab("Expected quantiles") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 6.5),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 5.5), legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2)))
rm(tmpDat)
```

```{r qqplotsVicari, fig.cap = "QQ-plots of observed p-values (y-axis) versus standard uniform quantiles (x-axis) for nonparametric simulations based on the Vicari data, for different methods (colour) when permuting observations of one or two modalities (columns) of the sections (rows). The solid black line has slope 1 and intercept 0.\\label{fig:qqVicari}", fig.height=8}
moltRealVicari <- melt(realFitsScrambleVicariPrep, id.vars = "featurePair")
colnames(moltRealVicari)[4:5] <- c("Method", "rep")
moltRealVicari <- cbind(moltRealVicari, vicariGrid[moltRealVicari[, "rep"], ])
moltRealVicari$what <- factor(moltRealVicari$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealVicari$Method <- factor(moltRealVicari$Method,
  levels = methLevels,
  labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
moltRealVicariAgg <- aggregate(data = moltRealVicari, x = value ~ Method + sample + what, FUN = getPower)
moltRealVicariAgg$Dataset <- "Vicari"
ggplot(data = moltRealVicari[sample(nrow(moltRealVicari), nrow(moltRealVicari) / subSamQQ), 1:7], aes(sample = value, col = Method)) +
  geom_qq(distribution = stats::qunif, size = 0.25) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(what), rows = vars(sample)) +
  ylab("Observed quantiles") +
  xlab("Expected quantiles") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 6.5),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 5.5), legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2)))
rm(moltRealVicari)
```

```{r boxplotMoransIGodfrey, fig.cap = paste("Boxplots of univariate Moran's I (y-axis) of the", numFeatsGodfrey, "most abundant features per section (columns) in the Godfrey data. The dotted horizontal line indicates 0.\\label{fig:boxplotMoransIgodfrey}"), fig.height = 3.5}
moranIsGodfrey <- melt(lapply(realFitsMoranUniGodfrey, function(x) x[c("IxyStx", "IxyMsi")]))
colnames(moranIsGodfrey) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "MoransI", "Modality", "Sample")
moranIsGodfrey <- moranIsGodfrey[moranIsGodfrey$Var1 == moranIsGodfrey$Var2, ] # Only univariate Moran's I
moranIsGodfrey$Modality <- factor(moranIsGodfrey$Modality,
  levels = c("IxyStx", "IxyMsi"),
  labels = c("Transcriptome", "Metabolome")
)
ggplot(moranIsGodfrey, aes(y = MoransI, x = Modality, fill = Modality)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_grid(~Sample) +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 6.1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  ylab("Univariate Moran's I")
```

```{r boxplotMoransIVicari, fig.height = 3.5, fig.cap = paste("Boxplots of univariate Moran's I (y-axis) of the", numFeatsVicariScramble, "most abundant features per section (columns) in the Vicari data. The dotted horizontal line indicates 0.\\label{fig:boxplotMoransIvicari}")}
moranIsVicari <- melt(lapply(realFitsMoranUniVicari, function(x) x[c("IxyStx", "IxyMsi")]))
colnames(moranIsVicari) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "MoransI", "Modality", "Sample")
moranIsVicari$Modality <- factor(moranIsVicari$Modality,
  levels = c("IxyStx", "IxyMsi"),
  labels = c("Transcriptome", "Metabolome")
)
moranIsVicari <- moranIsVicari[moranIsVicari$Var1 == moranIsVicari$Var2, ]
ggplot(moranIsVicari, aes(y = MoransI, x = Modality, fill = Modality)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_grid(~Sample) +
  xlab("") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 5.2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  ylab("Univariate Moran's I")
```

```{r moransIincurringfalseVicariPrep}
moransIincurringVicari <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(scrambleVec[1:2], function(what) {
  morName <- switch(what,
    "Stx" = "IxyMsi",
    "Met" = "IxyStx"
  ) # Take non-permuted modality
  tmp <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(id <- which(vicariGrid$what == what), function(i) {
    Sam <- vicariGrid[i, "sample"]
    uniMoran <- diag(realFitsMoranUniVicari[[Sam]][[morName]])
    names(uniMoran) <- make.names(names(uniMoran))
    base::do.call(rbind, lapply(selfName(names(realFitsScrambleVicariPrep[[i]])), function(nn) {
      yy <- realFitsScrambleVicariPrep[[i]][[nn]]
      featNames <- sapply(strsplit(yy$featurePair, split = "__"), function(x) {
        x[switch(what,
          "Stx" = 2,
          "Met" = 1
        )]
      })
      boo <- tapply(yy$value, featNames, function(x) sum(x < sigLevel, na.rm = TRUE))
      data.frame(
        "findings" = boo, "id" = i,
        "MoransI" = uniMoran[names(boo)], "Method" = nn
      )
    }))
  }))
  tmp2 <- aggregate(findings ~ MoransI + id + Method, data = tmp, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  cbind(tmp2, vicariGrid[tmp2$id, ])
}))
moransIincurringVicari$what <- factor(moransIincurringVicari$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
```

```{r moransIincurringfalseVicari, fig.cap = "Predicted probability of detection as significant finding (y-axis) as a function of univariate Moran's I (x-axis) for different methods (colour), non-permuted modalities (columns) and dataset (rows) in the nonparametric simulation, modeled by a generalized linear mixed model with binomial outcome distribution (see text for details). The predictions are only shown within the range of observed univariate Moran's I values. The horizontal dotted line indicates the significance level of 0.05.\\label{fig:moransIuniFindings}", fig.height = 4.5}
moransIincurringVicari$negFindings <- numFeatsGodfrey - moransIincurringVicari$findings
fitsMoranGodfrey <- tapply(selfName(seq_len(nrow(moransIincurringGodfrey))), as.character(moransIincurringGodfrey$Method),
  FUN = function(i) {
    glmer(cbind(findings, negFindings) ~ MoransI * what + (1 | sample), family = binomial(), data = moransIincurringGodfrey[i, ])
  }
)
fitsMoranVicari <- tapply(selfName(seq_len(nrow(moransIincurringVicari))), as.character(moransIincurringVicari$Method),
  FUN = function(i) {
    glmer(cbind(findings, negFindings) ~ MoransI * what + (1 | sample), family = binomial(), data = moransIincurringVicari[i, ])
  }
)
predGridGod <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(moransIincurringGodfrey$what), function(what) {
  Range <- range(moransIincurringGodfrey$MoransI[moransIincurringGodfrey$what == what])
  data.frame("MoransI" = seq(Range[1], Range[2], length.out = 200), "what" = what)
}))
# Limit to observed univariate Moran's I
fitsMoranGodfreyPred <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(names(fitsMoranGodfrey), function(x) {
  data.frame(predGridGod,
    Dataset = "Godfrey", Method = x,
    "Probability_of_detection" = predict(fitsMoranGodfrey[[x]], newdata = predGridGod, re.form = NA, type = "response")
  )
}))
predGridVic <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(moransIincurringVicari$what), function(what) {
  Range <- range(moransIincurringVicari$MoransI[moransIincurringVicari$what == what])
  data.frame("MoransI" = seq(Range[1], Range[2], length.out = 200), "what" = what)
}))
fitsMoranVicariPred <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(names(fitsMoranVicari), function(x) {
  data.frame(predGridVic,
    Dataset = "Vicari", Method = x,
    "Probability_of_detection" = predict(fitsMoranVicari[[x]], newdata = predGridVic, re.form = NA, type = "response")
  )
}))
fitsMoranGodfreyPred <- factorizeDf(fitsMoranGodfreyPred)
fitsMoranVicariPred <- factorizeDf(fitsMoranVicariPred)
ggplot(data = rbind(fitsMoranGodfreyPred, fitsMoranVicariPred), aes(y = Probability_of_detection, x = MoransI, col = Method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .35) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = powerColours) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Dataset), cols = vars(what), scales = "free_x") +
  ylab("Probability of detection") +
  xlab("Univariate Moran's I") +
  theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8.5), strip.text.y = element_text(size = 8.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted")
rm(moransIincurringVicari, fitsMoranVicariPred, fitsMoranGodfreyPred, fitsMoranVicari, fitsMoranGodfrey)
```

\clearpage

```{r overallPlotNonPar, include = FALSE}
moltRealAll <- rbind(moltRealGodfreyExistAggAll, moltRealVicariAgg)
moltRealAllAgg <- aggregate(data = moltRealAll[moltRealAll$Method %in% methLabelsShow, ], value ~ what + Dataset + Method, FUN = mean)
ggplot(data = moltRealAllAgg, aes(x = Method, y = value, col = Method)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.1) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(what), cols = vars(Dataset), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  ylab("Type I error") +
  xlab("") +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted") +
  (ThemeSims <- theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10.5),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10.5),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13)
  ))
ggsave("GraphsPaper/nonParAll.pdf", height = 5.5)
```

```{r rmNonpar}
rm(realFitsScrambleVicariPrep, godfreyGrid, moltRealGodfreyAgg, moransIincurringGodfrey, moltRealGodfreyExistAggAll, moltRealVicariAgg, realFitsMoranUniGodfrey, realFitsMoranUniVicari, vicariGrid, realFitsScrambleGodfreyPrep, realFitsScrambleVicari, scrambleGodfreyFdr, distMat, moranIsGodfrey, moltRealAll, predGridGod, predGridVic, moranIsVicari, moltRealAllAgg, ThemeSims)
```

\clearpage

#### Multi-image analysis

```{r vicariMultiScrambleRes, fig.cap = "Type I error (y-axis) of different methods (colour) and weighting schemes (x-axis) for multi-image analysis of the Godfrey and Vicari data (columns) after scrambling one or two modalities (rows).\\label{fig:nonparMulti}", fig.height = 5.1, fig.width = 8}
GodfreyGroups <- selfName(c("BC", "LC"))
nScramsGodfrey <- 2e1
numFeatsGodfrey <- 3e1
scrambleGodfreyGrid <- expand.grid(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "group" = GodfreyGroups,
  "what" = c("Stx", "Met", "Stx_Met"), "rep" = seq_len(nScramsGodfrey)
)
if (!file.exists(scrambleFitGodfreyMultiFile <- "Results/scrambleFitsGodfreyMulti.RData")) {
  godfreyFilteredAll <- lapply(GodfreyGroups, function(x) {
    StxFeats <- filterFeatsList(lapply(godfrey[grep(x, samplesGodfrey)], function(y) y$Stx), numFeatsGodfrey)
    MsiFeats <- filterFeatsList(lapply(godfrey[grep(x, samplesGodfrey)], function(y) y$Msi), numFeatsGodfrey)
    list(
      "Stx" = lapply(godfrey[grep(x, samplesGodfrey)], function(y) y$Stx[, StxFeats]),
      "Msi" = lapply(godfrey[grep(x, samplesGodfrey)], function(y) y$Msi[, MsiFeats]),
      "Coord" = lapply(godfrey[grep(x, samplesGodfrey)], function(y) y$Coord)
    )
  })
  formRep <- formula(out ~ 1)
  scrambleFitsGodfreyMulti <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(scrambleGodfreyGrid)), function(i) {
    what <- scrambleGodfreyGrid[i, "what"]
    gr <- scrambleGodfreyGrid[i, "group"]
    rep <- scrambleGodfreyGrid[i, "rep"]
    id <- grep(gr, samplesGodfrey)
    StxList <- godfreyFilteredAll[[gr]]$Stx
    MetList <- godfreyFilteredAll[[gr]]$Msi
    CoordList <- godfreyFilteredAll[[gr]]$Coord
    if (grepl("Stx", what)) {
      StxList <- lapply(StxList, function(x) {
        tmp <- x[sample(nrow(x)), ]
        rownames(tmp) <- rownames(x)
        tmp
      })
    }
    if (grepl("Met", what)) {
      MetList <- lapply(MetList, function(x) {
        tmp <- x[sample(nrow(x)), ]
        rownames(tmp) <- rownames(x)
        tmp
      })
    }
    MoransI_sbivar <- sbivar(
      X = StxList, Y = MetList, Cx = CoordList, Ey = CoordList, findVariances = TRUE,
      method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
    )
    splineRes <- sbivar(
      X = StxList, Y = MetList, Cx = CoordList, Ey = CoordList, findVariances = TRUE,
      method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE, families = FamiliesGodfrey
    )
    corTest <- sbivar(X = StxList, Y = MetList, method = "Correlation", verbose = FALSE)
    # Fit linear models
    MoransI_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      verbose = FALSE,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      Formula = formRep,
      inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = FALSE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_weighted_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE,
      scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    GAM_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes, Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    GAM_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes, Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, verbose = FALSE))
    Cor_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(corTest, Formula = formRep, verbose = FALSE, inverseWeigh = FALSE))
    outList <- lapply(list(
      "MoransI_raw" = MoransI_raw, "MoransI_weighted" = MoransI_weighted,
      "MoransI_weighted_scaled" = MoransI_weighted_scaled, "MoransI_scaled" = MoransI_scaled,
      "GAM_raw" = GAM_raw, "GAM_weighted" = GAM_weighted, "Cor_raw" = Cor_raw
    ), function(x) x$result$Intercept)
    outList
  })
  save(file = scrambleFitGodfreyMultiFile, scrambleFitsGodfreyMulti)
} else {
  load(scrambleFitGodfreyMultiFile)
}
scrambleVicariGrid <- expand.grid(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "what" = c("Stx", "Met", "Stx_Met"),
  "rep" = seq_len(nScramblesVicari)
)
indivMouse <- sapply(sampleNamesMouse, substr, 1, 10)
designVicari <- data.frame("mouse" = indivMouse)
if (!file.exists(scrambleFitVicariMultiFile <- "Results/scrambleFitsVicariMulti.RData")) {
  StxFeats <- filterFeatsList(exprObjs[sampleNamesMouse], numFeatsVicariScramble)
  MetFeats <- filterFeatsList(msiObjs[sampleNamesMouse], numFeatsVicariScramble)
  MetList <- lapply(msiObjs[sampleNamesMouse], function(x) x[, intersect(colnames(x), MetFeats), drop = FALSE])
  StxList <- lapply(exprObjs[sampleNamesMouse], function(x) as.matrix(x[, StxFeats]))
  CoordStxList <- lapply(magpieCoords[sampleNamesMouse], function(x) x$Stx)
  CoordMetList <- lapply(magpieCoords[sampleNamesMouse], function(x) x$Msi)
  formRep <- formula(out ~ mouse)
  scrambleFitsVicariMulti <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(scrambleVicariGrid)), function(i) {
    what <- scrambleVicariGrid[i, "what"]
    rep <- scrambleVicariGrid[i, "rep"]
    if (grepl("Stx", what)) {
      StxList <- lapply(StxList, scrambleRows)
    }
    if (grepl("Met", what)) {
      MetList <- lapply(MetList, scrambleRows)
    }
    MoransI_sbivar <- sbivar(
      X = StxList, Y = MetList, Cx = CoordStxList,
      Ey = CoordMetList, findVariances = TRUE, normX = "rel", normY = "rel",
      method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
    )
    splineRes <- sbivar(
      X = StxList, Y = MetList, Cx = CoordStxList,
      Ey = CoordMetList, findVariances = TRUE,
      method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE, families = FamiliesVicari
    )
    # Fit linear models
    MoransI_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      verbose = FALSE,
      designDf = designVicari, Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designVicari,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = FALSE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_weighted_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designVicari,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    MoransI_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designVicari,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    GAM_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes,
      designDf = designVicari, Formula = formRep,
      inverseWeigh = FALSE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    GAM_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes,
      designDf = designVicari, Formula = formRep,
      inverseWeigh = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ))
    outList <- lapply(list(
      "MoransI_raw" = MoransI_raw, "MoransI_weighted" = MoransI_weighted,
      "MoransI_weighted_scaled" = MoransI_weighted_scaled, "MoransI_scaled" = MoransI_scaled,
      "GAM_raw" = GAM_raw, "GAM_weighted" = GAM_weighted
    ), function(x) x$result$Intercept)
    outList
  })
  save(scrambleFitsVicariMulti, file = scrambleFitVicariMultiFile)
} else {
  load(scrambleFitVicariMultiFile)
}
scramGodMultMolt <- melt(lapply(scrambleFitsGodfreyMulti, function(x) lapply(x, function(y) y[, "pVal"])))
colnames(scramGodMultMolt) <- c("pvalue", "Method", "replic")
scramGodMultMolt <- cbind(scramGodMultMolt, scrambleGodfreyGrid[scramGodMultMolt$replic, ])
scramVicMultMolt <- melt(lapply(scrambleFitsVicariMulti, function(x) lapply(x, function(y) y[, "pVal"])))
colnames(scramVicMultMolt) <- c("pvalue", "Method", "replic")
scramVicMultMolt <- cbind(scramVicMultMolt, scrambleVicariGrid[scramVicMultMolt$replic, ])
scramVicMultMolt$group <- "Vicari"
scramAllMultMoltAgg <- aggregate(data = rbind(scramGodMultMolt, scramVicMultMolt), pvalue ~ Method + group + what, FUN = getPower)
scramAllMultMoltAgg$what <- factor(scramAllMultMoltAgg$what,
  levels = scrambleVec,
  labels = scrambleLabels, ordered = TRUE
)
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight <- "Raw"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "scaled")] <- "Scaled"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "weighted")] <- "Weighted"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "weighted") & grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "scaled")] <- "Scaled and weighted"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight <- factor(scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight, levels = c("Raw", "Scaled", "Weighted", "Scaled and weighted"), ordered = TRUE)
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "Moran")] <- "MoransI_sbivar"
