scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "GAM")] <- "GAM"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method[grepl(x = scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, pattern = "Cor")] <- "PearsonCor"
scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method <- factor(scramAllMultMoltAgg$Method, levels = methLevels, labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE)
scramAllMultMoltAgg$group <- factor(scramAllMultMoltAgg$group, levels = c(GodfreyGroups, "Vicari"), labels = c("Godfrey: breast cancer", "Godrey: lung cancer", "Vicari: mouse"), ordered = TRUE)
ggplot(
  scramAllMultMoltAgg[scramAllMultMoltAgg$Weight %in% c("Raw", "Scaled", "Weighted"), ],
  aes(x = Weight, y = pvalue, col = Method)
) +
  geom_jitter(width = .12) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(what), cols = vars(group)) +
  ylab("Type I error") +
  xlab("") +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7.5), legend.position = "top")
rm(scramVicMultMolt, scrambleFitsVicariMulti, scramAllMultMoltAgg, scrambleFitsGodfreyMulti, scramGodMultMolt)
baa <- gc(verbose = FALSE)
```

\clearpage

### Parametric simulations

#### Single-image analysis

\fref{fig:MoransIVar} demonstrates empirically that our variance estimator $tr(\mb W^t \bs \Sigma_x \mb W \bs \Sigma_y)$ is accurate, whereas the variance estimator assuming i.i.d. observations ($tr(\mb W^t \mb W)$ as in *SpatialDM* by \textcite{Li2023} and by \textcite{DeTomaso2021}) underestimates the variance in presence of SAC. \fref{fig:qqParSim} shows qq-plots of the p-values for all methods the null scenarios. \fref{fig:PearsonTypeIerror} demonstrates how Pearson correlation and other methods suffer from type I error inflation only when there is SAC in both variables. \fref{fig:timingsN} shows average time consumption for the different methods on simulated datasets in the "no spatial pattern" scenario with joint coordinate sets for the number of spots sizes increasing as `r samSizesJoint`, over 20 replicates, on an Intel Core i5-11400H 2.70GHz processor.

```{r moranVariances, fig.cap = "Empirical variances of bivariate Moran's I (y-axis, dashed black line) as a function of number of spots (x-axis), decay parameter of the weight matrix (colums) and spatial pattern under the independence null (rows). The black dots indicate the estimated variance under the randomisation null, the brown dots are \\sbivar's estimates under the independence null. The y-axis is on the log scale.\\label{fig:MoransIVar}"}
jointGridAltNull <- expand.grid(stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "samSize" = samSizesJoint, "spatPats" = spatPatsShow[1:2])
if (!file.exists(jointNewMoransFile <- "Results/JointNewResMoran.RData")) {
  jointResNewMoran <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(jointGridAltNull)), function(ii) {
    spatPat <- jointGridAltNull[ii, "spatPats"]
    n <- jointGridAltNull[ii, "samSize"]
    xMat <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
    dat <- makeXY(xMat, xMat,
      what = spatPat, nSims = 30,
      nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
    )
    MoransI <- sbivar(dat$x,
      returnSEsMoransI = TRUE,
      dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
    )$result
    obsMoran <- MoransI[, !colnames(MoransI) %in% c("pVal", "pAdj")]
    indepVars <- sapply(eval(formals(sbivar:::sbivarSingle)$etas), function(eta) {
      wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(xMat, xMat, wo = "Gauss", eta = eta)
      sum(wm^2)
    })
    names(indepVars) <- colnames(obsMoran)[1:3]
    list("obsMoran" = obsMoran, "indepVars" = indepVars)
  })
  save(jointResNewMoran, file = jointNewMoransFile)
} else {
  load(jointNewMoransFile)
}
GPid <- grep("GP", jointGridAltNull$spatPat)
jointResNewMoran[GPid] <- lapply(jointResNewMoran[GPid], function(x) {
  x$obsMoran <- x$obsMoran[getSameNamesId(rownames(x$obsMoran)), ]
  x
})
indepVars <- melt(lapply(seq_along(jointResNewMoran), function(x) cbind(jointResNewMoran[[x]]$indepVars) / (jointGridAltNull[x, "samSize"] - 1)^2))
colnames(indepVars) <- c("what", "foo", "value", "rep")
indepVarsMolt <- cbind(indepVars, jointGridAltNull[indepVars[, "rep"], ])
trueVars <- melt(lapply(jointResNewMoran, function(x) cbind(apply(x$obsMoran[, 1:3], 2, var))))
colnames(trueVars) <- c("what", "foo", "value", "rep")
trueVarsMolt <- cbind(trueVars, jointGridAltNull[trueVars[, "rep"], ])
estVars <- melt(lapply(jointResNewMoran, function(x) cbind(colMeans(x$obsMoran[, 4:6]^2))))
colnames(estVars) <- c("what", "foo", "value", "rep")
estVarsMolt <- cbind(estVars, jointGridAltNull[estVars[, "rep"], ])
estVarsMolt$what <- gsub("SE\\(Ixy\\)_", "", estVarsMolt$what)
trueVarsMolt$what <- gsub("Ixy_", "", trueVarsMolt$what)
indepVarsMolt$what <- gsub("Ixy_", "", indepVarsMolt$what)
estVarsMolt$spatPats <- factor(estVarsMolt$spatPats, levels = spatPats, labels = spatPatsLabels, ordered = TRUE)
trueVarsMolt$spatPats <- factor(trueVarsMolt$spatPats, levels = spatPats, labels = spatPatsLabels, ordered = TRUE)
indepVarsMolt$spatPats <- factor(indepVarsMolt$spatPats, levels = spatPats, labels = spatPatsLabels, ordered = TRUE)
etas <- eval(formals(sbivar:::sbivarSingle)$etas)
estVarsMolt$what <- factor(estVarsMolt$what, levels = etas, labels = etas, ordered = TRUE)
trueVarsMolt$what <- factor(trueVarsMolt$what, levels = etas, labels = etas, ordered = TRUE)
indepVarsMolt$what <- factor(indepVarsMolt$what, levels = etas, labels = etas, ordered = TRUE)
ggplot(data = estVarsMolt, aes(x = samSize, y = value)) +
  geom_point(col = powerColours["Moran's I (independence) [sbivar]"]) +
  facet_grid(spatPats ~ what, scales = "free_y") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) +
  geom_line(data = trueVarsMolt, linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(data = indepVarsMolt, col = "black") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  xlab("Number of spots") +
  ylab("Var(I_xy)")
```

```{r simJointExist}
if (!file.exists(jointFile <- "Results/JointRes.RData")) {
  xMat <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
  distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(xMat, xMat)))
  wm <- sbivar:::buildWeightMat(xMat, xMat, wo = "Gauss", eta = etaSim)
  tp <- ppp(xMat[, "x"], xMat[, "y"])
  listW <- mat2listw(wm, style = "M")
  listWgeary <- listw2U(listW)
  dgr <- as.matrix(spatialreg::as_dgRMatrix_listw(listWgeary))
  idDgr <- which(dgr != 0)
  wc <- spweights.constants(listWgeary, zero.policy = FALSE, adjust.n = TRUE)
  prepCzaplewski <- prepareCzaplewskiWeights(wm)
  H <- diag(n) - 1 / n
  ns <- n / sum(wm)
  fac2 <- (n / ((n - 1) * sum(wm)))^2
  IxyvarSpatialDMSqrt <- sqrt(fac2 * tr(crossprod(H %*% wm %*% H))) # Crossproduct as in code rather than square as in supplementary
  xSeq <- seq(xMin, xMax, length.out = sqrt(n))
  jointRes <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(jointGrid)), function(ii) { #
    spatPat <- jointGrid[ii, "spatPats"]
    rep <- jointGrid[ii, "rep"]
    dat <- makeXY(xMat, xMat,
      what = spatPat, distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * (1 - propFeatNull),
      nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
    )
    datNull <- makeXY(xMat, xMat,
      what = "none", distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * propFeatNull,
      nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
    )
    colnames(dat$x) <- paste0(colnames(dat$x), "_TPx")
    colnames(dat$y) <- paste0(colnames(dat$y), "_TPy")
    dat$x <- cbind(dat$x, datNull$x)
    dat$y <- cbind(dat$y, datNull$y)
    xScaled <- scale(dat$x, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    yScaled <- scale(dat$y, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    ResExist <- loadBalanceBplapply(seq_len(nrow(featGrid)), function(bb) {
      i <- featGrid[bb, "i"]
      j <- featGrid[bb, "j"]
      ## Czaplewski
      pValCza <- {
        Ixy <- as.vector(dat$x[, i] %*% wm %*% dat$y[, j])
        resCza <- getVarCzaplewski(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], prepCzaplewski)
        makePval((Ixy - resCza["EIxy"]) / sqrt(resCza["varIxy"]))
      }
      ## SpatialDM
      pValSpatialDM <- {
        xCen <- xScaled[, i]
        yCen <- yScaled[, j]
        Ixynorm <- ns * as.vector(xCen %*% wm %*% yCen) / sqrt(sum(xCen^2) * sum(yCen^2))
        makePval(Ixynorm / IxyvarSpatialDMSqrt) # Checked with python code
      }
      ## Permutation
      IxyPvalPerm <- {
        permIxy <- vapply(integer(nPerms), FUN.VALUE = Ixy, function(jjj) {
          as.vector(dat$x[sample(n), i] %*% wm %*% dat$y[sample(n), j])
        })
        getPermPval(Ixy, permIxy)
      }
      # SpaGene
      spaGenePval <- SpaGene_LR(expr = rbind("x" = dat$x[, i], "y" = dat$y[, j]), location = xMat, LRpair = data.frame("x" = "x", "y" = "y"), normalize = FALSE, perm = nPerms)[["pval"]]
      # Lee's L, using spdep
      leeObj <- lee.test(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], listW)$p.value
      leeObjPerm <- lee.mc(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], listW, nsim = nPerms)$p.value
      # Geary's C
      gearCperm <- {
        obsGeary <- geary.bi.fast(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], wc, dgr)
        outerMat <- outer(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], "-")^2
        permGeary <- wc$n1 / wc$S0 * vapply(integer(nPerms), FUN.VALUE = obsGeary, function(jjj) {
          sum(dgr[idDgr] * outerMat[sample(n), sample(n)][idDgr])
        })
        getPermPval(obsGeary, permGeary)
      }
      # Pearson correlation:
      pearRes <- cor.test(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j])$p.value
      # Random shift test
      CCres <- CCtestWrapper(
        x = dat$x[, i], y = dat$y[, j], xSeq = xSeq, N.shifts = nPerms,
        ySeq = xSeq, tp = tp
      )$p.value
      out <- rbind(
        "MoransI_Czaplewski" = pValCza,
        "MoransI_SpatialDM" = pValSpatialDM,
        "MoransI_permutation" = IxyPvalPerm,
        "SpaGene" = spaGenePval, "LeesL_asymptotic" = leeObj,
        "LeesL_permutation" = leeObjPerm, "GearysC_permutation" = gearCperm,
        "PearsonCor" = pearRes, "RandomShift" = CCres
      )
      colnames(out) <- c("pVal")
      out
    })
    # Random shift Moran's I
    CCresMoran <- CCtestEtas(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, xMat, nPerms, verbose = FALSE)
    CCresMoranP <- c(CCresMoran)
    names(CCresMoranP) <- apply(expand.grid(rownames(CCresMoran), colnames(CCresMoran)), 1, paste, collapse = "__")
    MoransI <- sbivar(MoransI_sbivar <- dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(MoransI) <- makeRowNames(MoransI)
    gpRes <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "GP", verbose = TRUE)$result
    rownames(gpRes) <- makeRowNames(gpRes)
    splineRes <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(splineRes) <- makeRowNames(splineRes)
    splineResGAMM <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, Gamm = TRUE, method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(splineResGAMM) <- makeRowNames(splineResGAMM)
    modtTest <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Modif", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(modtTest) <- makeRowNames(modtTest)
    pVals <- cbind(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = MoransI[, "pVal"], "GAM" = splineRes[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"],
      "GAMM" = splineResGAMM[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"],
      "modTtest" = modtTest[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"], "MoransI_RandomShift" = CCresMoranP[rownames(MoransI)]
    )
    gamCors <- splineRes[, c("corxy", "se.corxy")]
    ResNew <- list("pVal" = pVals, "gamCors" = gamCors)
    list("Exist" = ResExist, "New" = ResNew)
  })
  save(jointRes, file = jointFile)
} else {
  load(jointFile)
}
```

```{r parExistRes}
spatLabsNull <- c("No spatial pattern", "Independent GPs")
featGridPower <- expand.grid("i" = c(paste0(seq_len(nFeats * (1 - propFeatNull)), "TP"), seq_len(nFeats * propFeatNull)), "j" = c(paste0(seq_len(nFeats * (1 - propFeatNull)), "TP"), seq_len(nFeats * propFeatNull)))
namesTmp <- paste0("feat", featGridPower$i, "X__", "feat", featGridPower$j, "Y")
moltExistOld <- melt(lapply(jointRes, function(x) {
  tmp <- x$Exist
  names(tmp) <- namesTmp
  lapply(tmp, function(xx) {
    xx[, "pVal", drop = FALSE]
  })
}))
colnames(moltExistOld) <- c("Method", "what", "value", "featurePair", "rep")
moltExistNew <- melt(lapply(jointRes, function(x) x$New$pVal))
colnames(moltExistNew) <- c("featurePair", "Method", "value", "rep")
moltExist <- rbind(moltExistOld[, c("featurePair", "Method", "value", "rep")], moltExistNew)
moltExist <- cbind(moltExist[, c("featurePair", "Method", "value")], jointGrid[moltExist[, "rep"], ])
moltExist <- factorizeDf(moltExist)
rm(moltExistNew, moltExistOld)
moltExistSplitFeat <- simplify2array(strsplit(moltExist$featurePair, "__"))
moltExistSplitFeat[1, ] <- gsub("_", "", gsub("x", "", gsub("X", "", moltExistSplitFeat[1, ])))
moltExistSplitFeat[2, ] <- gsub("_", "", gsub("y", "", gsub("Y", "", moltExistSplitFeat[2, ])))
moltExist$TP <- ((moltExist$spatPats != "Dependent GPs") | (moltExistSplitFeat[1, ] == moltExistSplitFeat[2, ])) & (str_count(moltExist$featurePair, "TP") == 2) & !(moltExist$spatPats %in% spatLabsNull)
moltExistPaggPower <- aggregate(moltExist[moltExist$TP, ],
  value ~ Method + spatPats,
  FUN = getPower
)
moltExistPaggPower$what <- "Power"
moltExistPaggTypeIerror <- aggregate(moltExist[!moltExist$TP, ],
  value ~ Method + spatPats,
  FUN = getPower
)
moltExistPaggTypeIerror$what <- "Type I error"
rm(moltExistSplitFeat)
moltExist <- droplevels(moltExist)
dfAll <- rbind(moltExistPaggTypeIerror, moltExistPaggPower)
dfAll$what <- factor(dfAll$what, levels = c("Type I error", "FDR", "Power", "Sensitivity", "FDR_fdrtool", "Sensitivity_fdrtool"), ordered = TRUE)
```

```{r qqplotParSim, fig.cap = "QQ-plots of observed p-values (y-axis) versus standard uniform quantiles (x-axis) for parametric simulations under the two null scenarios (columns), for different methods (colour). The solid black line has slope 1 and intercept 0.\\label{fig:qqParSim}", fig.height = 9}
id <- which(moltExist$spatPats %in% c("No spatial pattern", "Independent GPs"))
ggplot(
  data = moltExist[sample(id, length(id / 5)), ],
  aes(sample = value, col = Method)
) +
  geom_qq(distribution = stats::qunif, size = 0.4) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Method), cols = vars(spatPats)) +
  ylab("Observed quantiles") +
  xlab("Expected quantiles") +
  xlim(c(0, 1)) +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  theme(
    strip.text.y = element_blank(), strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7.5),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 7), legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.25)))
rm(id)
```

```{r falsePosPearsonCor, fig.height = 4.75, fig.width = 8, fig.cap = "Type I error (y-axis) for different analysis methods (colour) as a function of whether there is no SAC, SAC in one or in both variables (x-axis), for outcomes generated as independent GPs. The horizontal dotted line indicates the significance level of 5\\%.\\label{fig:PearsonTypeIerror}"}
namesTmp <- paste0("feat", featGridPower$i, "X__", "feat", featGridPower$j, "y")
moltExistOldPear <- melt(lapply(jointRes, function(x) {
  tmp <- t(sapply(x$Exist, function(y) y[, "pVal"]))
  rownames(tmp) <- namesTmp
  tmpNew <- x$New$pVal
  list(old = tmp, new = tmpNew)
}))
colnames(moltExistOldPear) <- c("featurePair", "Method", "value", "foo", "rep")
moltExistOldPear$value <- as.numeric(moltExistOldPear$value)
moltExistSplitFeat <- simplify2array(strsplit(as.character(moltExistOldPear[, "featurePair"]), "__"))
moltExistOldPear$TP <- grepl("TP", moltExistSplitFeat[1, ]) + grepl("TP", moltExistSplitFeat[2, ])
moltExistOldPear <- cbind(moltExistOldPear[, c("value", "TP", "Method")], jointGrid[moltExistOldPear[, "rep"], ])
moltExistOldPearAgg <- aggregate(moltExistOldPear,
  value ~ spatPats + TP + Method,
  FUN = getPower
)
moltExistOldPearAgg$TP <- factor(moltExistOldPearAgg$TP,
  levels = 0:2,
  labels = c("No SAC", "SAC in\none variable", "SAC in\nboth variables"), ordered = TRUE
)
moltExistOldPearAgg <- factorizeDf(moltExistOldPearAgg)
ggplot(data = moltExistOldPearAgg[moltExistOldPearAgg$spatPats == "Independent GPs" & moltExistOldPearAgg$Method %in% methLabelsShow, ], aes(y = value, x = TP, col = Method)) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(spatPats), scale = "free_y") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = powerColours) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted") +
  ylab("Type I error") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.36, size = 1) +
  xlab("") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7.5), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 8)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 4))
```

```{r rmStuff}
rm(estVarsMolt, jointResNewMoran, jointRes, aggDf, estVars, indepVars, indepVarsMolt, moltExist, moltExistOldPear, moltExistOldPearAgg, moltExistPaggFdr, moltExistPaggFDR, moltExistPaggPower, moltExistPaggTypeIerror, moltExistSplitFeat, moltFdr, moltTim, moltTimAgg, newGrid, secondGrid, timingsGrid, trueVars, trueVarsMolt)
```

```{r disjointsim}
if (!file.exists(disFile <- "Results/disJointRes.RData")) {
  xMat <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
  yMat <- makeCoordGrid(m, xMin + 0.03, xMax + 0.03)
  distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(xMat, yMat)))
  idnn_XY <- nn2(yMat, xMat, k = 1)$nn.idx[, 1]
  wm <- buildWeightMat(xMat, xMat, wo = "Gauss", eta = etaSim)
  prepCzaplewski <- prepareCzaplewskiWeights(wm)
  ns <- n / sum(wm)
  fac2 <- (n / ((n - 1) * sum(wm)))^2
  H <- diag(n) - 1 / n
  IxyvarSpatialDMSqrt <- sqrt(fac2 * tr(crossprod(H %*% wm %*% H)))
  listW <- mat2listw(wm, style = "M")
  listWgeary <- listw2U(listW)
  dgr <- as.matrix(spatialreg::as_dgRMatrix_listw(listWgeary))
  idDgr <- which(dgr != 0)
  wc <- spweights.constants(listWgeary, zero.policy = FALSE, adjust.n = TRUE)
  xSeq <- seq(xMin, xMax, length.out = sqrt(n)) # Crossproduct as in code rather than square as in supplementary
  tp <- ppp(xMat[, "x"], xMat[, "y"])
  disjointRes <- mclapply(seq_len(nrow(jointGrid)), mc.cores = nCores, function(ii) {
    spatPat <- jointGrid[ii, "spatPats"]
    rep <- jointGrid[ii, "rep"]
    dat <- makeXY(xMat, yMat, what = spatPat, distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * (1 - propFeatNull), nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs)
    datNull <- makeXY(xMat, yMat,
      what = if (spatPat %in% c("dependentStreakGPs", "dependentZonesGPs")) "independentGPs" else "none",
      distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * propFeatNull,
      nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
    )
    colnames(dat$x) <- paste0(colnames(dat$x), "_TPx")
    colnames(dat$y) <- paste0(colnames(dat$y), "_TPy")
    dat$x <- cbind(dat$x, datNull$x)
    dat$y <- cbind(dat$y, datNull$y)
    gpRes <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y,
      Cx = xMat,
      Ey = yMat, method = "GP", verbose = FALSE
    )$result[, "pVal"]
    MoransI_sbivar <- sbivar(dat$x, Ey = yMat, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE)$result
    splineRes <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, Ey = yMat, method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE)$result
    splineResGAMM <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, Ey = yMat, Gamm = TRUE, method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE)$result
    MoransI_permutation <- {
      c(MoransIpermutation(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, nPerms = nPerms))
    }
    CCres <- {
      c(CCtestEtas(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, nPerms))
    }
    namesMat <- sbivar:::makeNames(colnames(dat$x), colnames(dat$y))
    names(MoransI_permutation) <- names(CCres) <- namesMat
    pVals <- cbind(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = MoransI[, "pVal"], "GAM" = splineRes[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"],
      "GAMM" = splineResGAMM[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"], "modTtest" = modtTest[rownames(MoransI), "pVal"], "MoransI_RandomShift" = CCresMoranP[rownames(MoransI)]
    )
    # Match observations
    dat$y <- dat$y[idnn_XY, ]
    xScaled <- scale(dat$x, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    yScaled <- scale(dat$y, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
    ResExist <- t(vapply(seq_len(nrow(featGrid)), FUN.VALUE = double(7), function(bb) {
      i <- featGrid[bb, "i"]
      j <- featGrid[bb, "j"]
      ## Czaplewski
      pValCza <- {
        Ixy <- as.vector(dat$x[, i] %*% wm %*% dat$y[, j])
        resCza <- getVarCzaplewski(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], prepCzaplewski)
        makePval((Ixy - resCza["EIxy"]) / sqrt(resCza["varIxy"]))
      }
      ## SpatialDM
      pValSpatialDM <- {
        xCen <- xScaled[, i]
        yCen <- yScaled[, j]
        Ixynorm <- ns * as.vector(xCen %*% wm %*% yCen) / sqrt(sum(xCen^2) * sum(yCen^2))
        makePval(Ixynorm / IxyvarSpatialDMSqrt) # Checked with python code
      }
      # SpaGene
      spaGenePval <- SpaGene_LR(expr = rbind("x" = dat$x[, i], "y" = dat$y[, j]), location = xMat, LRpair = data.frame("x" = "x", "y" = "y"), normalize = FALSE, perm = nPerms)[["pval"]]
      # Lee's L, using spdep
      leeObj <- lee.test(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], listW)$p.value
      # Geary's C
      gearCperm <- {
        obsGeary <- geary.bi.fast(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], wc, dgr)
        outerMat <- outer(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j], "-")^2
        permGeary <- wc$n1 / wc$S0 * vapply(integer(nPerms), FUN.VALUE = obsGeary, function(jjj) {
          sum(dgr[idDgr] * outerMat[sample(n), sample(n)][idDgr])
        })
        getPermPval(obsGeary, permGeary)
      }
      # Pearson correlation:
      pearRes <- cor.test(dat$x[, i], dat$y[, j])$p.value
      # Random shift test
      CCres <- CCtestWrapper(x = dat$x[, i], y = dat$y[, j], xSeq = xSeq, ySeq = xSeq, tp = tp, N.shifts = nPerms)$p.value
      c("MoransI_Czaplewski" = pValCza, "MoransI_SpatialDM" = pValSpatialDM, "SpaGene" = spaGenePval, "LeesL_asymptotic" = leeObj, "GearysC_permutation" = gearCperm, "PearsonCor" = pearRes, "RandomShift" = CCres)
    }))
    rownames(ResExist) <- namesTmp
    modtTest <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Modif", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(modtTest) <- makeRowNames(modtTest)
    out <- cbind(ResExist, "modTtest" = modtTest[namesTmp, "pVal"])
    list("pVal" = cbind(pVals, out[rownames(pVals), ]))
  })
  save(disjointRes, file = disFile)
} else {
  load(disFile)
}
```

```{r parNewResDis, include = FALSE}
moltNewDis <- melt(lapply(disjointRes, function(x) x$pVal))
colnames(moltNewDis) <- c("featurePair", "Method", "value", "rep")
moltNewDis <- cbind(moltNewDis, jointGrid[moltNewDis[, "rep"], ])
moltNewDis <- factorizeDf(moltNewDis)
moltExistSplitFeat <- simplify2array(strsplit(as.character(moltNewDis$featurePair), "__"))
moltExistSplitFeat[1, ] <- gsub("_", "", gsub("X", "", gsub("x", "", moltExistSplitFeat[1, ])))
moltExistSplitFeat[2, ] <- gsub("_", "", gsub("Y", "", gsub("y", "", moltExistSplitFeat[2, ])))
moltNewDis$TP <- ((moltNewDis$spatPats != "Dependent GPs") | (moltExistSplitFeat[1, ] == moltExistSplitFeat[2, ])) & grepl(
  "TP",
  moltExistSplitFeat[1, ]
) & grepl("TP", moltExistSplitFeat[2, ]) & !(moltNewDis$spatPats %in% spatLabsNull)
moltNewDisaggPower <- aggregate(moltNewDis[moltNewDis$TP, ], value ~ Method + spatPats, FUN = getPower)
moltNewDisaggPower$what <- "Power"
moltNewDisaggT1E <- aggregate(moltNewDis[!moltNewDis$TP, ], value ~ Method + spatPats, FUN = getPower)
moltNewDisaggT1E$what <- "Type I error"
moltNewDisPagg <- rbind(moltNewDisaggT1E, moltNewDisaggPower)
moltNewDisPagg$what <- factor(moltNewDisPagg$what, levels = c("Type I error", "Power"), ordered = TRUE)
dfAll$Coordinates <- "Joint"
moltNewDisPagg$Coordinates <- "Disjoint"
dfAll2 <- rbind(dfAll, moltNewDisPagg)
dfAll2$Coordinates <- factor(dfAll2$Coordinates, levels = c("Joint", "Disjoint"), ordered = TRUE)
pp <- ggplot(data = dfAll2[dfAll2$Method %in% methLabelsShow, ], aes(col = Method, y = value, x = Method, group = Method)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = powerColours) +
  facet_nested(rows = vars(Coordinates, what), cols = vars(spatPats), scale = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted") +
  ylab("Type I error/power") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  xlab("") +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9.1),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 4))
ggsave(pp, file = "GraphsPaper/resAll.pdf", height = 8.25, width = 9.1)
rm(moltExist, pp, dfAll2, moltNewDis)
```

```{r timingsN, fig.cap = "Runtimes (in seconds) (y-axis) for different methods that control type I error rate (colour) and increasing number of spots (x-axis) with joint coordinate sets in the 'No spatial pattern' scenario. Bivariate GPs and GAMMs were not run for the higher spot numbers.\\label{fig:timingsN}", fig.height = 3.25, fig.width = 6.5}
dir.nw(timingsFolder <- "Results/timings/")
samSizesJoint <- c(20^2, 25^2, 30^2, 35^2, 40^2, 45^2)
timingsGrid <- expand.grid("samSize" = samSizesJoint, "rep" = seq_len(10))
if (!file.exists(timingsNFile <- "Results/JointResNTimings.RData")) {
  nSimsTime <- 10
  register(SerialParam())
  timingsN <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(timingsGrid)), function(i) {
    if (!file.exists(timFile <- paste0(timingsFolder, "timing", i, ".RData"))) {
      n <- timingsGrid[i, "samSize"]
      rep <- timingsGrid[i, "rep"]
      xMat <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
      dat <- makeXY(xMat, xMat, what = "none", nSims = nSimsTime)
      MoransI_sbivar <- peakRAM(sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE))
      GAMs <- peakRAM(sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE))
      GAMMs <- if (n <= samSizesJoint[2]) {
        peakRAM(sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "GAMs", Gamm = TRUE, verbose = FALSE))
      } else {
        rep(NA, 4)
      }
      GPs <- if (n <= samSizesJoint[3]) {
        peakRAM(sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "GPs", verbose = FALSE))
      } else {
        rep(NA, 4)
      }
      modTtest <- peakRAM(sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, method = "Modified", verbose = FALSE))
      MoransI_RandomShift <- peakRAM(CCtestEtas(dat$x, dat$y, Cx = xMat, Ey = xMat, nPerms = 1e3, verbose = FALSE))
      out <- t(rbind("MoransI_sbivar" = MoransI_sbivar[c(2, 4)], "GAM" = GAMs[c(2, 4)], "GAMM" = GAMMs[c(2, 4)], "GP" = GPs[c(2, 4)], "modTtest" = modTtest[c(2, 4)], "MoransI_RandomShift" = MoransI_RandomShift[c(2, 4)]))
      save(out, file = timFile)
    } else {
      load(timFile)
    }
    out
  })
  save(timingsN, file = timingsNFile)
} else {
  load(timingsNFile)
}
moltTim <- melt(timingsN)
colnames(moltTim) <- c("what", "Method", "value", "repl")
moltTim$what <- factor(moltTim$what, levels = timeLevels, labels = timeLabels, ordered = TRUE)
moltTim <- cbind(moltTim, timingsGrid[moltTim[, "repl"], ])
moltTim$Method <- factor(moltTim$Method, levels = methLevels, labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE)
moltTimAgg <- aggregate(moltTim, value ~ Method + what + samSize, FUN = mean)
ggplot(data = moltTimAgg[moltTimAgg$what == "Runtime (in seconds)", ], aes(x = samSize, y = value, col = Method)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  xlab("Number of spots") +
  ylab("Runtimes (seconds)")
rm(moltTim, moltTimAgg, timingsGrid, timingsN)
```

```{r jitterOutcomedistrs}
if (!file.exists(xMatsFile <- "Results/xMats.RData")) {
  xMats <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sam) {
    visiumCoords <- read.csv(header = FALSE, paste0("Data/Vicari2024/Vicari2024_Magpie/input/", sam, "/visium/spatial/tissue_positions_list.csv"), col.names = c("barcode", "in_tissue", "array_row", "array_col", "x", "y"))
    as.matrix(visiumCoords[visiumCoords$in_tissue == 1, c("x", "y")])
  })
  yMats <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sam) {
    msiCoords <- read.csv(paste0(
      "Data/Vicari2024/Vicari2024_Magpie/", sam,
      "/spaceranger/spatial/tissue_positions_list.csv"
    ), row.names = 1, header = FALSE)[, 1:3]
    colnames(msiCoords) <- c("in_tissue", "x", "y")
    as.matrix(msiCoords[msiCoords$in_tissue == 1, c("x", "y")])
  })
  save(xMats, yMats, sampleNamesMouse, file = xMatsFile)
}
jitterGrid <- expand.grid(stringsAsFactors = FALSE, "spatPats" = spatPatsShow, "rep" = seq_len(nSimsJitter), "sdJitter" = sdJitter, "sample" = sampleNamesMouse)
NugsJitter <- c(0.85, 0.85)
RangsJitter <- c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1)
if (!file.exists(jitterFile <- "Results/jitterDistr.RData")) {
  load(xMatsFile)
  jitterRes <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(jitterGrid)), function(ii) {
    spatPat <- jitterGrid[ii, "spatPats"]
    rep <- jitterGrid[ii, "rep"]
    sam <- jitterGrid[ii, "sample"]
    sd <- jitterGrid[ii, "sdJitter"]
    moved <- sbivar:::moveTwoCoords(xMats[[sam]], yMats[[sam]])
    xMatGen <- moved$Cx
    yMatGen <- moved$Ey
    distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(xMatGen, yMatGen)))
    dat <- makeXY(xMatGen, yMatGen,
      what = spatPat, distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * (1 - propFeatNull),
      nuggets = NugsJitter, ranges = RangsJitter, sigmas = Sigs, scaleZone = 0.01,
      widthStreak = 0.05, boundsBetas = c(0.3, 0.5)
    )
    colnames(dat$x) <- paste0(colnames(dat$x), "_TPx")
    colnames(dat$y) <- paste0(colnames(dat$y), "_TPy")
    datNull <- makeXY(xMatGen, yMatGen,
      what = if (spatPat %in% c("dependentStreakGPs", "dependentZonesGPs")) "independentGPs" else "none", distMat = distMat, nSims = nFeats * propFeatNull,
      nuggets = NugsJitter, ranges = RangsJitter, sigmas = Sigs
    )
    dat$x <- cbind(dat$x, datNull$x)
    dat$y <- cbind(dat$y, datNull$y)
    rownames(dat$x) <- rownames(xMatGen)
    rownames(dat$y) <- rownames(yMatGen)
    if (sd == 0) {
      xMat <- xMatGen
      yMat <- yMatGen
    } else {
      yMat <- as.matrix(read.csv(paste0(lmFolder, "newCoords_rep", rep, "sd", sd, "sample", sam, ".csv"), header = FALSE)[, c(3, 4)])
      dimnames(yMat) <- list(paste0("samY", seq_len(nrow(yMat))), c("x", "y"))
      moved <- sbivar:::moveTwoCoords(xMats[[sam]], yMat)
      xMat <- moved$Cx
      yMat <- moved$Ey
      dimnames(yMat) <- dimnames(yMatGen)
    }
    Moranres <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, method = "Moran", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(Moranres) <- paste0(Moranres[, "Modality_X"], "__", Moranres[, "Modality_Y"])
    GAMres <- sbivar(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, method = "GAM", verbose = FALSE)$result
    rownames(GAMres) <- paste0(GAMres[, "Modality_X"], "__", GAMres[, "Modality_Y"])
    MoransI_permutation <- c(MoransIpermutation(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, nPerms = nPerms, verbose = FALSE))
    CCres <- c(CCtestEtas(dat$x, dat$y, xMat, yMat, nPerms, verbose = FALSE))
    names(MoransI_permutation) <- names(CCres) <- sbivar:::makeNames(colnames(dat$x), colnames(dat$y))
    pVals <- cbind(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = Moranres[, "pVal"],
      "MoransI_permutation" = MoransI_permutation[rownames(Moranres)],
      "GAM" = GAMres[rownames(Moranres), "pVal"],
      "MoransI_RandomShift" = CCres[rownames(Moranres)]
    )
    pVals
  })
  save(jitterRes, file = jitterFile)
} else {
  load(jitterFile)
}
```

```{r jitterProc, fig.cap = "Type I error (two left panels) and power (five right panels) (y-axis) averaged over all nine Vicari data template sections as a function of standard deviation by which the landmarks were jittered (x-axis) for different methods (colour), spatial patterns (columns). A standard deviation of 0 corresponds to an analysis using the true, unjittered coordinates.\\label{fig:jitter}", fig.height = 5, fig.width = 7}
id <- which(sapply(jitterRes, is.matrix))
jitterMolt <- do.call(what = rbind, lapply(
  id, # seq_along(jitterRes),
  function(i) {
    tmp <- jitterRes[[i]]
    TP <- if (jitterGrid$spatPats[[i]] %in% c("none", "independentGPs")) {
      rep(FALSE, nrow(tmp))
    } else if (jitterGrid$spatPats[[i]] == "dependentGPs") {
      getSameNamesId(rownames(tmp), which = FALSE)
    } else {
      str_count(rownames(tmp), pattern = "TP") == 2
    }
    data.frame(
      "pVal" = c(tmp), "Method" = rep(colnames(tmp), each = nrow(tmp)), "TP" = rep(TP, times = ncol(tmp)),
      jitterGrid[i, -2, drop = FALSE], row.names = NULL
    )
  }
))
jitterMoltAgg <- aggregate(data = jitterMolt, x = pVal ~ Method + spatPats + sdJitter + TP, FUN = getPower)
jitterMoltAgg <- factorizeDf(jitterMoltAgg)
jitterMoltAgg$TP <- factor(jitterMoltAgg$TP, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("Type I error", "Power"), ordered = TRUE)
ggplot(data = jitterMoltAgg, aes(y = pVal, x = sdJitter, col = Method)) +
  facet_grid(TP ~ spatPats) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = powerColours) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sdJitter) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dashed") +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
  ylab("Type I error/power") +
  xlab("Jitter standard deviation") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top", strip.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 6.7), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6.1)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sdJitter, labels = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3))
rm(jitterMolt, jitterMoltAgg, jitterRes, jitterGrid)
```

\clearpage

#### Multi-image analysis

```{r repSimSamSize}
designDfRep <- data.frame("mouse" = indivMouse)
formRep <- formula(~ (1 | mouse))
repReps <- 50
nSimsReps <- 15
repGrid <- expand.grid("rep" = seq_len(repReps), "spatPat" = spatPatsShow, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
numZones <- 6
if (!file.exists(repFileSS <- "Results/repSimStudySamSize.RData")) {
  repSimsSamSize <- mclapply(seq_len(nrow(repGrid)), mc.cores = nCores, function(ii) {
    w <- repGrid[ii, "spatPat"]
    rep <- repGrid[ii, "rep"]
    betas <- matrix(runif(2 * nSimsReps * (1 - propFeatNull), 0.5, 1), nSimsReps * (1 - propFeatNull), 2)
    betaZones <- matrix(runif(numZones * nSimsReps * (1 - propFeatNull), 2, 2.5), nSimsReps * (1 - propFeatNull), numZones)
    datList <- lapply(selfName(seq_len(9)), function(sn) {
      n <- max(rnbinom(1, mu = sqrt(n), size = 5)^2, 1e2)
      m <- max(rnbinom(1, mu = sqrt(m), size = 5)^2, n)
      Cx <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
      Ey <- makeCoordGrid(m, xMin + 0.03, xMax + 0.03)
      idnn_XY <- nn2(Cx, Ey, k = 1)$nn.idx[, 1]
      distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(Cx, Ey)))
      dat <- makeXY(Cx, Ey, what = w, distMat = distMat, nSims = nSimsReps * (1 - propFeatNull), nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs, betas = betas, betaZonesX = betaZones)
      datNull <- makeXY(Cx, Ey,
        what = if (w %in% c("dependentStreakGPs", "dependentZonesGPs")) "independentGPs" else "none",
        distMat = distMat, nSims = nSimsReps * propFeatNull, nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
      )
      colnames(dat$x) <- paste0(colnames(dat$x), "_TPx")
      colnames(dat$y) <- paste0(colnames(dat$y), "_TPy")
      dat$x <- cbind(dat$x, datNull$x)
      dat$y <- cbind(dat$y, datNull$y)
      c(dat, list("Cx" = Cx, "Ey" = Ey, "idnn_XY" = idnn_XY))
    })
    Xl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$x)
    Yl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$y)
    Cxl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$Cx)
    Eyl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$Ey)
    MoransI_sbivar <- sbivar(
      X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = TRUE,
      method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
    )
    splineRes <- sbivar(
      X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = TRUE,
      method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE
    )
    corTest <- sbivar(
      X = lapply(datList, function(x) x$x[x$idnn_XY, ]),
      Y = Yl, method = "Correlation", verbose = FALSE
    )
    # Fit linear models
    MoransI_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    MoransI_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = FALSE, verbose = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    MoransI_weighted_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    MoransI_scaled <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(MoransI_sbivar,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    GAM_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, verbose = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    GAM_weighted <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(splineRes,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE, verbose = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    Cor_raw <- extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(corTest,
      designDf = designDfRep,
      Formula = formRep, verbose = FALSE, inverseWeigh = FALSE
    ), designDf = designDfRep)
    outList <- lapply(list(
      "MoransI_raw" = MoransI_raw, "MoransI_weighted" = MoransI_weighted,
      "MoransI_weighted_scaled" = MoransI_weighted_scaled, "MoransI_scaled" = MoransI_scaled,
      "GAM_raw" = GAM_raw, "GAM_weighted" = GAM_weighted, "Cor_raw" = Cor_raw
    ), function(x) x$result$Intercept)
    outList
  })
  save(repSimsSamSize, file = repFileSS)
} else {
  load(repFileSS)
}
```

```{r procSepPlot, fig.cap = "Type I error and power (y-axis, rows) for different methods (colours) and different spatial patterns (columns) in multi-image parametric simulations, as a function of weighing scheme used in the linear model (x-axis).\\label{fig:repSims}", fig.height=4.5, fig.weight = 8}
moltPowersSep <- melt(lapply(repSimsSamSize, function(x) lapply(x, function(y) data.frame("featurePair" = makeRowNames(y[, 1:2]), y[, "pVal"]))), id.vars = "featurePair")[, -2]
names(moltPowersSep) <- c("featurePair", "value", "Method", "run")
moltPowersSep <- cbind(moltPowersSep, repGrid[moltPowersSep[, "run"], ])
moltPowersSep$what <- factor(((moltPowersSep$spatPat != "dependentGPs") | getSameNamesId(moltPowersSep$featurePair, which = FALSE)) & (str_count(moltPowersSep$featurePair, "TP") == 2) & !(moltPowersSep$spatPat %in% spatLabsNull), levels = c(TRUE, FALSE), labels = c("Power", "Type I error"))
moltPowersSep$spatPat <- factor(moltPowersSep$spatPat, levels = spatPats, labels = spatPatsLabels, ordered = TRUE)
moltPowersSep$Weight <- "Raw"
moltPowersSep$Weight[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "scaled")] <- "Scaled"
moltPowersSep$Weight[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "weighted")] <- "Weighted"
moltPowersSep$Weight[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "weighted") & grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "scaled")] <- "Scaled and weighted"
moltPowersSep$Weight <- factor(moltPowersSep$Weight, levels = c("Raw", "Scaled", "Weighted", "Scaled and weighted"), ordered = TRUE)
moltPowersSep$Method[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "Moran")] <- "MoransI_sbivar"
moltPowersSep$Method[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "GAM")] <- "GAM"
moltPowersSep$Method[grepl(x = moltPowersSep$Method, pattern = "Cor")] <- "PearsonCor"
moltPowersSep$Method <- factor(moltPowersSep$Method, levels = methLevels, labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE)
ReplicateAgg <- aggregate(x = formula("value ~ Method + Weight + spatPat + what"), data = moltPowersSep, FUN = getPower)
ggplot(data = ReplicateAgg[ReplicateAgg$Weight %in% c("Raw", "Scaled", "Weighted"), ], aes(col = Method, x = Weight, y = value)) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(spatPat), rows = vars(what)) +
  geom_point() +
  xlab("Weighing scheme") +
  ylab("Power/Type I error") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90), strip.text = element_text(size = 7), legend.position = "top") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = sigLevel, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = powerColours)
rm(moltPowersSep, repSimsSamSize)
```

```{r rmparsims}
rm(
  xMat, yMat, dfAll, dfAll2, disjointRes, featGrid, featGridPower, jointGrid, jointGridAltNull,
  moltExistSplitFeat, moltNewDisaggPower, moltNewDisaggT1E, moltNewDisPagg, repGrid, ReplicateAgg, scrambleGodfreyGrid, scrambleVicariGrid
)
baa <- gc(verbose = FALSE)
```

```{r timingsRep}
nSimsTime <- 2e1
nRepsTime <- 10
if (!file.exists(repFileTimings <- "Results/repSimStudyTimings.RData")) {
  register(SerialParam())
  repSimsTiming <- lapply(seq_len(nRepsTime), function(rep) {
    cat(rep)
    datList <- lapply(selfName(seq_len(9)), function(sn) {
      n <- max(rnbinom(1, mu = sqrt(n), size = 5)^2, 1e2)
      m <- max(rnbinom(1, mu = sqrt(m), size = 5)^2, n)
      Cx <- makeCoordGrid(n, xMin, xMax)
      Ey <- makeCoordGrid(m, xMin + 0.03, xMax + 0.03)
      idnn_XY <- nn2(Cx, Ey, k = 1)$nn.idx[, 1]
      distMat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(rbind(Cx, Ey)))
      dat <- makeXY(Cx, Ey,
        what = "none", nSims = nSimsTime, distMat = distMat,
        nuggets = Nugs, ranges = Rangs, sigmas = Sigs
      )
      c(dat, list("Cx" = Cx, "Ey" = Ey, "idnn_XY" = idnn_XY))
    })
    Xl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$x)
    Yl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$y)
    Cxl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$Cx)
    Eyl <- lapply(datList, function(x) x$Ey)
    MoransI_sbivar_raw <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = FALSE, findMaxW = FALSE,
        method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = FALSE, scaleByMax = FALSE
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    MoransI_sbivar_scaled <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = FALSE, findMaxW = TRUE,
        method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    MoransI_sbivar_weighted <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = TRUE, findMaxW = TRUE,
        method = "Moran's I", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    splineRes_raw <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl,
        method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    splineRes_weighted <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = Xl, Y = Yl, Cx = Cxl, Ey = Eyl, findVariances = TRUE,
        method = "GAMs", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep, inverseWeigh = TRUE
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    corTest <- system.time({
      res <- sbivar(
        X = lapply(datList, function(x) x$x[x$idnn_XY, ]),
        Y = Yl, method = "Correlation", verbose = FALSE
      )
      extractResultsMulti(fitLinModels(res,
        designDf = designDfRep, verbose = FALSE,
        Formula = formRep
      ), designDf = designDfRep)
    })[3]
    c("MoransI_sbivar_raw" = MoransI_sbivar_raw, "MoransI_sbivar_scaled" = MoransI_sbivar_scaled, "MoransI_sbivar_weighted" = MoransI_sbivar_weighted, "GAM_raw" = splineRes_raw, "GAM_weighted" = splineRes_weighted, "PearsonCor" = corTest)
  })
  save(repSimsTiming, file = repFileTimings)
} else {
  load(repFileTimings)
}
```

```{r plottimingsrep, fig.height = 3, fig.width = 5, fig.cap = "Average runtimes (in seconds) (y-axis) for different methods (colour) and scaling schemes (x-axis) applied to multiple images with disjoint coordinate sets in the 'No spatial pattern' scenario of parametric simulations over 10 Monte-carlo instances with 20 features per modality.\\label{fig:timingsRep}"}
repSimsTiming <- do.call(what = rbind, lapply(seq_along(repSimsTiming), function(x) data.frame("Method" = c("MoransI_sbivar", "MoransI_sbivar", "MoransI_sbivar", "GAM", "GAM", "PearsonCor"), "Scaling" = c("Raw", "Scaled", "Weighted", "Raw", "Weighted", "Raw"), "time" = repSimsTiming[[x]], "repl" = x)))
repSimsTiming$Method <- factor(repSimsTiming$Method, levels = methLevels, labels = methLabels, ordered = TRUE)
repSimsTiming$group <- paste0(repSimsTiming$Method, repSimsTiming$Scaling)
repSimsTimingAgg <- aggregate(repSimsTiming, time ~ Method + Scaling, FUN = mean)
ggplot(data = repSimsTimingAgg, aes(col = Method, x = Scaling, y = time)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = powerColours) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  xlab("Method") +
  ylab("Runtime (seconds)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90), strip.text = element_text(size = 7))
rm(repSimsTimingAgg, repSimsTiming, repSimsTiming, designDfRep)
```

\fref{fig:repSims} shows type I error and power for different methods under replication, proving the added value of scaling the Moran's I statistic by its maximum absolute value, but suggesting that inverse weighting by variance is not a good strategy. \fref{fig:timingsRep} shows a timing comparison on an Intel Core i5-11400H 2.70GHz processor.

\clearpage

# Real datasets

## Godfrey et al. (2025)

The dataset by \textcite{Godfrey2025} contains seven sections from human lung and breast cancer tissue coprofiled using desorption electrospray ionisation mass spectrometry imaging (DESI-MSI) for metabolites, followed by H&E staining and finally Visium for transcripts. The data were pre-aligned the help of the H&E stain and spot-matched by aggregating the metabolomics data with a Gaussian weighting scheme. \fref{fig:GodfreyLibs} reveals that the library sizes and total ion counts exhibit spatial patterning across the sections.

```{r LC3libsizes, fig.cap = "Log10 spot-wise sums (colour) of the different sections (rows) of the Godfrey data.\\label{fig:GodfreyLibs}", fig.height = 9}
foo <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(x) {
  dfStx <- data.frame(godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Coord, "log10_Library_size" = log10(rowSums(godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Stx)), what = "Transcriptomics")
  dfMsi <- data.frame(godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Coord, "log10_Library_size" = log10(rowSums(godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Msi)), what = "Metabolomics")
  df <- rbind(dfStx, dfMsi)
  df$sample <- x
  df
}))
foo$what <- factor(foo$what, levels = c("Transcriptomics", "Metabolomics"), ordered = TRUE)
ggplot(data = foo, aes(x = x, y = y, col = log10_Library_size)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.5, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(sample ~ what, space = "free", scales = "free") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 6.7)) +
  xlab("Coordinate 1") +
  ylab("Coordinate 2") +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "yellow", high = "blue", name = "Log10(spot sum)") +
  coord_fixed()
FamiliesGodfrey <- list("X" = mgcv::nb(), "Y" = Gamma(lin = "log"))
```

### Single-image analysis

```{r godfreyreal}
if (!file.exists(realFitGodfreyRegFile <- "Results/realFitsGodfreyRegistered.RData")) {
  realFitsGodfrey <- lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sn) {
    dat <- godfreyIdentified[[sn]]
    Moranres <- sbivar(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord, method = "Moran", normX = "rel", normY = "rel")
    GAMres <- sbivar(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, dat$Coord, families = FamiliesGodfrey, method = "GAM")
    CorRes <- corTests(dat$Stx, dat$Msi, normX = "rel", normY = "rel")[, "pVal"]
    list("MoransI_sbivar" = Moranres, "GAM" = GAMres, "PearsonCor" = CorRes)
  })
  save(realFitsGodfrey, file = realFitGodfreyRegFile)
}
if (!file.exists(realFitGodfreyRegFileSmall <- "Results/realFitsGodfreyRegisteredSmall.RData")) {
  load(realFitGodfreyRegFile)
  realFitsGodfrey <- lapply(realFitsGodfrey, function(x) {
    list(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = x$MoransI_sbivar$result[, c(7, 8), drop = FALSE],
      "GAM" = x$GAM$result[, c("corxy", "pVal", "pAdj"), drop = FALSE],
      "PearsonCor" = cbind("pAdj" = p.adjust(x$PearsonCor, method = "BH"))
    )
  })
  save(realFitsGodfrey, file = realFitGodfreyRegFileSmall)
} else {
  load(realFitGodfreyRegFileSmall)
}
```

\clearpage

#### Comparison to authors' findings

```{r foundByGambutnotoriginally, include = FALSE}
godSize <- 0.5
foundOnlyGam <- lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sam) {
  foundOriginal <- godfreyMetTable[godfreyMetTable$Sample == names(mappingSamples)[mappingSamples == sam], ]
  originalPairs <- make.names(paste0(foundOriginal$Genes, "__", foundOriginal$MZmatched))
  gamRes <- realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$GAM
  gamRes <- gamRes[gamRes[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel, , drop = FALSE]
  newPairs <- setdiff(rownames(gamRes), originalPairs)
  oldPairs <- setdiff(y = rownames(gamRes), originalPairs)
  list("onlyGAM" = newPairs, "onlyOld" = oldPairs)
})
onlyGamTopPairs <- sapply(foundOnlyGam, function(x) x$onlyGAM[1])
moranTT <- t(sapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sam) {
  realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$Moran[onlyGamTopPairs[sam], ]
}))
# Strong negative correlations for GAM
# Weak or none for correlations
id <- sapply(onlyGamTopPairs, is.character)
pGod <- plotGodfreyAll(pairsGod <- simplify2array(onlyGamTopPairs[id]), size = 0.32, samples = samplesGodfrey[id], stripSizeX = 5.35, xLimShift = 0.055, axisLegendSizeX = 6.5, legendShift = -90)
ggsave(pGod, file = "GraphsPaper/PairGodfrey.pdf", height = 8.5, width = 5)
rm(onlyGamTopPairs, pGod, foundOnlyGam)
foundOnlyGamPos <- lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sam) {
  foundOriginal <- godfreyMetTable[godfreyMetTable$Sample == names(mappingSamples)[mappingSamples == sam], ]
  originalPairs <- make.names(paste0(foundOriginal$Genes, "__", foundOriginal$MZmatched))
  gamRes <- realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$GAM
  setdiff(rownames(gamRes[(gamRes[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel) & (gamRes[, "corxy"] > 0), , drop = FALSE]), originalPairs)
})
onlyGamTopPairsPos <- sapply(foundOnlyGamPos, function(x) x[1])
moranTTpos <- t(sapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sam) {
  realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$Moran[onlyGamTopPairsPos[sam], ]
}))
rm(foundOnlyGamPos)
onlyGamTopPairsPos <- simplify2array(onlyGamTopPairsPos[sapply(onlyGamTopPairsPos, is.character)])
```

```{r gamposGod, fig.cap = paste("Pairs found most significantly positively correlated by GAMs in different sections of the Godfrey data (rows), but not detected in the original analysis.\\label{fig:gamsOnlyGodfreyPos}", pasteCA(rownames(moranTTpos)[moranTTpos[, "pAdj"]<sigLevel]), "were also found significant by bivariate Moran's I."), fig.height = 8.9}
plotGodfreyAll(pairs = onlyGamTopPairsPos, size = 0.37, samples = names(onlyGamTopPairsPos), xLimShift = 0.04)
topMetsPos <- getMets(onlyGamTopPairsPos)
topMetsPosCom <- allMetsCommon[topMetsPos]
```

We can confirm all highest correlations discussed by the authors in the main text of their original publication. Differences in findings with the full supplementary results are shown in \fref{fig:GAMGodfrey} in the main text and \fref{fig:gamsOnlyGodfreyPos} here for findings only by our methods, and Figs \ref{fig:gamsOnlyOriginal}-\ref{fig:gamsOnlyOriginal2} for findings in the original analysis but not by our methods.

```{r foundByGambutnotoriginallyPlot, fig.height = 5, fig.cap = "Pairs with highest correlation found in the original analysis, but not declared significant by bivariate Moran's I or GAMs.\\label{fig:gamsOnlyOriginal}"}
foundOnlyOrig <- lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(sam) {
  foundOriginal <- godfreyMetTable[godfreyMetTable$Sample == names(mappingSamples)[mappingSamples == sam], ]
  originalPairs <- make.names(paste0(foundOriginal$Genes, "__", foundOriginal$MZmatched))
  gamRes <- realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$GAM
  gamRes <- gamRes[(gamRes[, "pAdj"] > sigLevel), , drop = FALSE]
  MoranNotFinds <- rownames(realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$MoransI_sbivar)[realFitsGodfrey[[sam]]$MoransI_sbivar[, "pAdj"] > sigLevel]
  intersect(originalPairs, intersect(MoranNotFinds, rownames(gamRes))) # highest original correlation
})
foundOnlyOrig <- foundOnlyOrig[sapply(foundOnlyOrig, length) > 0]
onlyOrigTopPairs <- sapply(foundOnlyOrig, function(x) x[1])
idOrig <- !is.na(onlyOrigTopPairs)
plotGodfreyAll(pairs = gp1 <- simplify2array(onlyOrigTopPairs[idOrig]), size = godSize + 0.1, samples = samOrig <- names(onlyOrigTopPairs)[idOrig])
resPear <- t(mapply(realFitsGodfrey[samOrig], gp1, FUN = function(x, y) x$Pearson[y, ]))
# Not significant
resGAM <- t(mapply(realFitsGodfrey[samOrig], gp1, FUN = function(x, y) x$GAM[y, ])) # Weak correlation and not significant
ttt <- godfreyMetTable[(godfreyMetTable$MZmatched %in% getMets(gp1)) & (godfreyMetTable$Genes %in% getGenes(gp1)), ]
```

```{r foundByGambutnotoriginallyPlot2, fig.cap = "Pairs with second highest significant correlation found in the original analysis, but not declared significant by bivariate Moran's I or GAMs.\\label{fig:gamsOnlyOriginal2}", fig.height = 4.7}
onlyOrigTopPairs2 <- sapply(foundOnlyOrig, function(x) x[2])
idOrig <- !is.na(onlyOrigTopPairs2)
gp1 <- onlyOrigTopPairs2[idOrig]
plotGodfreyAll(pairs = gp1, size = godSize + 0.02, samples = names(gp1))
```

\clearpage

#### New findings

New findings by GAMs are shown in Figs \ref{fig:godfreySmallMoran}-\ref{fig:godfreyKLKs} and Table \ref{tab:klk}.

```{r godFreySmallestMoran, fig.cap = "Gene and metabolite (columns) most significantly associated per section (rows) according to the bivariate Moran's I in the Godfrey data. Colours reflect abundance, scaled to 0-1 for legibility.\\label{fig:godfreySmallMoran}", fig.height = 8}
smallestGodfrey <- lapply(realFitsGodfrey, function(yy) {
  lapply(yy, function(zz) {
    rownames(zz)[which.min(zz[, "pAdj"])]
  })
})
plotGodfreyAll(moranTop <- sapply(smallestGodfrey, function(x) x$MoransI_sbivar), samples = names(smallestGodfrey), size = 0.3)
gamTopPairs <- sapply(smallestGodfrey, function(x) x$GAM)
gamTopPairs <- simplify2array(gamTopPairs[sapply(gamTopPairs, length) > 0])
gamTopAccordingToMoran <- sapply(samplesGodfrey, function(x) {
  realFitsGodfrey[[x]]$MoransI_sbivar[gamTopPairs[x], "pAdj"]
})
```

```{r godFreySmallestGAM, fig.cap = paste0("Gene and metabolite (columns) most significant per section (rows) according to GAMs in the Godfrey data. The findings in ", pasteCA(names(gamTopAccordingToMoran)[gamTopAccordingToMoran<sigLevel]), " are also significant according to bivariate Moran's I. Colours reflect abundance, scaled to 0-1 for legibility.\\label{fig:godfreySmallGAM}"), fig.height = 8.2}
plotGodfreyAll(gamTopPairs, samples = names(gamTopPairs), size = 0.37, xLimShift = 0.05)
gamCommon <- allMetsCommon[getMets(gamTopPairs)]
```

```{r godFreySmallestGAMposKLK1, fig.cap = "Relative abundance of PI 34:1 and the kallikrein transcripts most strongly associated with it (columns) in samples of the Godfrey data (rows). Colours reflect abundance, scaled to 0-1 for legibility. See Table \\ref{tab:klk} for full results.\\label{fig:godfreyKLKs}", fig.height = 9.4}
smallestGodfreyGAMpos <- sapply(realFitsGodfrey[names(gamTopPairs)], function(yy) {
  rownames(yy$GAM)[yy$GAM[, "corxy"] > 0][1]
})
gamCommonPos <- allMetsCommon[getMets(smallestGodfreyGAMpos)]
Sam <- "LC_091"
KLKgenes <- paste0("KLK", 10:13)
met <- getMets(smallestGodfreyGAMpos)[1] # "PI 34:1"
dfFoo <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(Sam) {
  data.frame("Sample" = Sam, base::do.call(rbind, c(
    lapply(KLKgenes, function(x) {
      if (x %in% colnames(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Stx)) data.frame(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Coord, "what" = x, "value" = scaleZeroOne(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Stx[, x] / rowSums(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Stx)))
    }),
    list(data.frame(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Coord,
      "what" = "PI 34:1",
      "value" = scaleZeroOne(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Msi[, met] / rowSums(godfreyIdentified[[Sam]]$Msi))
    ))
  )))
}))
ggplot(data = dfFoo, aes(x = x, y = y, col = value)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.44, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(Sample ~ what, scales = "free", space = "free") +
  xlab("Coordinate 1") +
  ylab("Coordinate 2") +
  scale_colour_gradient(high = "blue", low = "yellow", name = "Relative\nabundance") +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90), legend.title = element_text(size = 7.5),
    legend.position = "top", strip.text = element_text(size = 7)
  )
rm(dfFoo)
```

```{r klks, results = "asis"}
klkGAMs <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(yy) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(realFitsGodfrey[[yy]]$GAM), paste0(KLKgenes, "__", met))
  baa <- realFitsGodfrey[[yy]]$GAM[tmp, ]
  colnames(baa)[1:3] <- c("Correlation", "P.value", "Adjusted.P.value")
  if (length(tmp)) data.frame("Sample" = yy, "Gene" = getGenes(tmp), baa[, c("Correlation", "Adjusted.P.value")])
}))
print.xtable(comment = FALSE, xtable(klkGAMs[klkGAMs[, "Adjusted.P.value"] < sigLevel, ], caption = "\\label{tab:klk}Significant correlations of \\textit{KLK} genes with PI 34:1 estimated by GAMs with adjusted p-values for all Godfrey samples. Only KLK10 in BC\\_515\\_Section\\_1 and KLK13 in LC\\_091 are also significant according to bivariate Moran's I.", display = c("s", "s", "s", "f", "e")), include.rownames = FALSE)
klkMorans <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(yy) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(realFitsGodfrey[[yy]]$Moran), paste0(KLKgenes, "__", met))
  data.frame("Sample" = yy, realFitsGodfrey[[yy]]$Moran[tmp, ])
}))
foo <- klkMorans[klkMorans[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel, ]
rm(klkGAMs, klkMorans, foo)
```

```{r godfreycancermasks, fig.cap = "Godfrey data sections (rows) coloured by pathologists' annotation of the spots.\\label{fig:GodfreyPatho}", fig.height = 9}
foo <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(samplesGodfrey, function(x) {
  df <- data.frame(godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Coord, "Annotation" = godfreyIdentified[[x]]$Annot)
  df$sample <- x
  df
}))
ggplot(data = foo, aes(x = x, y = y, col = Annotation)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.55, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(sample ~ ., scales = "free", space = "free") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8), axis.title = element_text(size = 9)) +
  xlab("Coordinate 1") +
  ylab("Coordinate 2") +
  coord_fixed() +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 1.25)))
rm(foo)
```

```{r rmgodfreyreal}
rm(godfreyIdentified, realFitsGodfrey)
```

\clearpage

### Multi-image analysis

In the original publication, the authors also look at which correlations are high in replicate sections of the same cancer type. They identify high correlations between *MUC1* and a series of phospholipids \parencite{Godfrey2025}, but these are not significant after multiplicity correction in our analysis with GAMs (\fref{fig:pvalHistGodfreyMulti}).

```{r godHists, fig.cap = "Histograms of p-values of GAMs of a replicated analysis per cancer type (plot titles) for the Godfrey data.\\label{fig:pvalHistGodfreyMulti}", fig.height = 4}
GodfreyGroups <- selfName(c("BC", "LC"))
GodfreyMethods <- selfName(c("GAM", "MoransI_sbivar"))
if (!file.exists(godMulfile <- "Results/godfreyMulti.RData")) {
  godMulti <- lapply(GodfreyGroups, function(gr) {
    id <- grep(gr, samplesGodfrey)
    unPairsTab <- table(unlist(lapply(realFitsGodfrey[id], function(x) {
      rownames(x[["GAM"]])
    })))
    unPairs <- names(unPairsTab[unPairsTab >= 2])
    out <- vapply(unPairs, FUN.VALUE = 0, function(up) {
      t.test(vapply(realFitsGodfrey[id], FUN.VALUE = 0, function(x) {
        if (up %in% rownames(x[["GAM"]])) x[["GAM"]][up, "corxy"] else NA
      }))$p.value
    })
  })
  save(godMulti, file = godMulfile)
} else {
  load(godMulfile)
}
pAdjGodMult <- lapply(godMulti, function(x) sort(p.adjust(x, method = "BH")))
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
foo <- lapply(GodfreyGroups, function(x) {
  hist(godMulti[[x]], xlab = "P-value", main = switch(x,
    "BC" = "Breast cancer",
    "LC" = "Lung cancer"
  ))
})
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
rm(godMulti, pAdjGodMult, foo, godfreyMetTable, smallestGodfrey)
```

## Vicari et al. (2024)

This is a dataset on spatial transcriptomics and metabolomics on the same tissue sections from mouse and human brain \parencite{Vicari2024}. Here we focus on the three mice were measured in triplicate. The first two triplets of mouse sections (V11L12-038 and V11L12-109) are from the striatum, the third (V11T16-085) is from the substantia nigra. The mice's brains were lesioned on one side through 6-hydroxydopamine (6-OHDA) injection to emulate Parkinson's disease. The authors aligned the images manually using the location information of the non-empty samples only with *STUtility* \parencite{Bergenstrahle2020}, but we used the *MAGPIE* package \parencite{Williams2026} to guide manual alignment using anchor points (available in supplementary material), the resulting alignment is shown in \fref{fig:magpieVicari}. The spatial distributions of the spot-wise sums (library sizes and total ion counts) are shown in \fref{fig:libSizeVicari}, revealing spatial patterns for them.

```{r loadWangjunMagpie, fig.cap = "Images from \\textcite{Vicari2024} aligned using \\textit{MAGPIE}. Metabolic spots are black, transcriptomic ones are blue. \\label{fig:magpieVicari}", fig.height = 12, fig.width = 10}
par(mfrow = c(5, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 3))
foo <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(x) {
  plotxy(pch = "+", magpieCoords[[x]]$Msi, magpieCoords[[x]]$Stx, main = x, cex = 0.5)
})
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r plotLibSize, fig.cap = "Spatial distribution of spot-wise sums (library sizes and total ion counts) from the transcriptomics respectively metabolomics modalities (columns) of the Vicari2024 data, for different sections (rows). All spot sums have been scaled to the [0,1] range per modality and omics type for legibility.\\label{fig:libSizeVicari}", fig.height = 8.8, fig.width = 6, dpi = 100}
foo <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(x) {
  data.frame(magpieCoords[[x]]$Stx,
    libSize = scaleZeroOne(rowSums(exprObjs[[x]])), what = "Transcriptomics",
    sample = x, image = x, replicate = 1 + which(x == sampleNamesMouse) %% 3
  )
}))
fooMet <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(x) {
  data.frame(magpieCoords[[x]]$Msi,
    libSize = scaleZeroOne(rowSums(msiObjs[[x]])), what = "Metabolomics",
    sample = x, image = x, replicate = 1 + which(x == sampleNamesMouse) %% 3
  )
}))
fooAll <- rbind(foo, fooMet)
fooAll$what <- factor(fooAll$what, levels = c("Transcriptomics", "Metabolomics"), ordered = TRUE)
ggplot(data = fooAll, aes(x = x, y = y, col = libSize)) +
  geom_point(shape = 18, size = 0.175) +
  facet_grid(image ~ what) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 6), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 6), axis.legend = element_text(size = 6)
  ) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "yellow", high = "blue", name = "Spot sums") +
  coord_fixed() +
  xlab("Coordinate 1") +
  ylab("Coordinate 2")
rm(foo, fooMet, fooAll)
```

\clearpage

### Single-image analysis

#### Comparison to authors' findings

In the original publication the authors used nearest neighbour distances to find a one-to-one mapping between metabolic and transcriptomic samples, for which then Pearson correlation is calculated \parencite{Vicari2024}. The investigation focused on the metabolite dopamine, for which positive and negative correlations with a number of genes were found, but some were not confirmed by our analysis based on bivariate Moran's I or GAMs (\fref{fig:vicariNotConfirmedStria}-\ref{fig:vicariNotConfirmedSN}). We noticed that bivariate Moran's I and GAMs are complementary, as the former struggles with heteroskedasticity, whereas the latter with sharp hotspots and edge effects.

```{r moransICons}
SubstantiaNigraGenes <- selfName(c("Th", "Slc6a3", "Slc18a2", "Ddc", "Dlk1", "Ret", "Slc10a4", "Snca", "Pvalb"))
StriataGenes <- selfName(c("Pcp4", "Tac1", "Hpca", "mt.Nd3", "Camkk2", "Camk2n1", "Atp2b1", "Penk", "Cartpt"))
numFeatsVicariReal <- 1e3
if (!file.exists(moranVicariFile <- "Results/VicariRes.RData")) {
  allResVicari <- lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sn) {
    yFeats <- intersect(unMetsIdentified, colnames(msiObjs[[sn]]))
    MsiDat <- normMat(msiObjs[[sn]], norm = "rel")[, yFeats]
    GAMres <- sbivar(as.matrix(exprObjs[[sn]]), msiObjs[[sn]], magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx,
      magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi,
      families = FamiliesVicari, method = "GAM", featuresY = yFeats
    )
    MoranRandomShift <- CCtestEtas(as.matrix(exprObjs[[sn]]), MsiDat, magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx,
      normY = "none",
      magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi, nPerms = nPerms, normX = "rel"
    )
    MoranResPerm <- MoransIpermutation(as.matrix(exprObjs[[sn]]), MsiDat, magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx,
      magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi,
      nPerms = nPerms, normX = "rel", normY = "none"
    )
    Moranres <- sbivar(as.matrix(exprObjs[[sn]]), msiObjs[[sn]], magpieCoords[[sn]]$Stx,
      featuresY = yFeats,
      magpieCoords[[sn]]$Msi, method = "Moran", normX = "rel", normY = "rel"
    )
    list(
      "MoransI_sbivar" = Moranres, "GAM" = GAMres,
      "MoranResPerm" = MoranResPerm, "MoranRandomShift" = MoranRandomShift
    )
  })
  save(allResVicari, file = moranVicariFile)
} else {
  load(moranVicariFile)
}
```

```{r compareToAuthorfindings}
Metab <- c("Dopamine" = "X674.2805")
SNsamples <- grep("V11T16-085", value = TRUE, sampleNamesMouse)
Striasamples <- grep("V11T16-085", value = TRUE, sampleNamesMouse, invert = TRUE)
GAMresDop <- sapply(allResVicari[Striasamples], function(xx) {
  Nam <- rownames(xx$GAM$result)
  baa <- sapply(StriataGenes, function(gene) {
    tmp <- xx$GAM$result$pVal[grepl(Metab, Nam) & grepl(paste0(gene, "__"), Nam)]
    if (length(tmp)) tmp else NA
  })
  baa
})
MoranresDop <- sapply(allResVicari[Striasamples], function(xx) {
  Nam <- rownames(xx$MoransI_sbivar$result)
  baa <- sapply(StriataGenes, function(gene) {
    tmp <- xx$MoransI_sbivar$result[grepl(Metab, Nam) & grepl(paste0(gene, "__"), Nam), "pVal"]
    if (length(tmp)) tmp else NA
  })
  baa
})
unconfirmedStriata <- intersect(rownames(GAMresDop)[apply(GAMresDop, 1, allNotSignif)], rownames(MoranresDop)[apply(MoranresDop, 1, allNotSignif)])
```

```{r normStuff}
exprObjsNorm <- lapply(exprObjs, function(x) normMat(x, "rel"))
rm(exprObjs)
msiObjsNorm <- lapply(msiObjs, normMat, "rel")
rm(GAMresDop, msiObjs)
foo <- gc()
```

```{r plotStria, fig.cap = "Genes (all but last row) found by the authors to be associated to dopamine (last row), but not significant for bivariate Moran's I or GAMs, in different sections of the striatum of the Vicari data where dopamine was detected (columns).\\label{fig:vicariNotConfirmedStria}", fig.height = 8.5}
plotVicariGenesMets(samples = grep("V11L12-038", Striasamples, invert = TRUE, value = TRUE), size = 0.54, mets = Metab, genes = unconfirmedStriata)
```

```{r plotSubstNigra, fig.cap = "Genes (all but last row) for which both bivariate Moran's I and GAMs find a different association to dopamine (last row) than the authors, in different substantia nigra sections of the Vicari data (columns). \\textit{Snca} was found positively associated by the authors but negatively associated or not significant by \\sbivar. \\textit{Pvalb} was found negatively associated by the authors but positively associated or not significant by \\sbivar. \\label{fig:vicariNotConfirmedSN}", fig.height = 8.5}
GAMresDopSN <- sapply(allResVicari[SNsamples], function(xx) {
  Nam <- rownames(xx$GAM$result)
  sapply(SubstantiaNigraGenes, function(gene) {
    tmp <- xx$GAM$result$pVal[grepl(Metab, Nam) & grepl(paste0(gene, "__"), Nam)]
    if (length(tmp)) unname(tmp) else NA # [tmp<sigLevel]
  })
})
GAMresDopSNcor <- sapply(allResVicari[SNsamples], function(xx) {
  Nam <- rownames(xx$GAM$result)
  sapply(SubstantiaNigraGenes, function(gene) {
    tmp <- xx$GAM$result$corxy[grepl(Metab, Nam) & grepl(paste0(gene, "__"), Nam)]
    if (length(tmp)) unname(tmp) else NA # [tmp<sigLevel]
  })
})
MoranresDopSN <- sapply(allResVicari[SNsamples], function(xx) {
  Nam <- rownames(xx$MoransI_sbivar$result)
  sapply(SubstantiaNigraGenes, function(gene) {
    tmp <- xx$MoransI_sbivar$result[grepl(Metab, Nam) & grepl(paste0(gene, "__"), Nam), "pVal"]
    if (length(tmp)) unname(tmp) else NA # [tmp<sigLevel]
  })
})
unconfirmedSN <- rownames(GAMresDopSN)[(apply(GAMresDopSN, 1, allNotSignif) & apply(MoranresDopSN, 1, allNotSignif)) | apply(GAMresDopSNcor, 1, function(x) all(x < 0, na.rm = TRUE))]
# Pvalb was found negatively associated
plotVicariGenesMets(samples = SNsamples, size = 0.43, mets = Metab, genes = unconfirmedSN)
rm(GAMresDopSN, unconfirmedSN, MoranresDopSN, GAMresDopSNcor)
```

```{r vicariMultiChunk}
vicariGroups <- selfName(c("Stria", "SN"))
if (!file.exists(realFitVicariMultiFile <- "Results/realFitsVicariMulti.RData")) {
  vicariMulti <- lapply(vicariGroups, function(x) {
    samples <- if (x == "Stria") Striasamples else SNsamples
    unPairsTab <- table(unlist(lapply(allResVicari[samples], function(x) {
      rownames(x[["GAM"]]$result)
    })))
    unPairs <- selfName(names(unPairsTab[unPairsTab >= 2]))
    out <- loadBalanceBplapply(unPairs, function(up) {
      gamCors <- vapply(allResVicari[samples], FUN.VALUE = 0, function(x) {
        if (up %in% rownames(x[["GAM"]]$result)) x[["GAM"]]$result[up, "corxy"] else NA
      })
      id <- !is.na(gamCors)
      fitGAM <- try(summary(lm(gamCors[id] ~ indivMouse[names(gamCors)[id]]))$coef["(Intercept)", c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")], silent = TRUE)
      if (inherits(fitGAM, "try-error")) {
        fitGAM <- try(summary(lm(gamCors[id] ~ 1))$coef["(Intercept)", c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")])
      }
      fitGAM
    })
    gamRes <- simplify2array(out[sapply(out, is.vector)])
    unPairsTabMoran <- table(unlist(lapply(allResVicari[samples], function(x) {
      rownames(x[["MoransI_sbivar"]]$result)
    })))
    unPairsMoran <- selfName(names(unPairsTabMoran[unPairsTabMoran >= 2]))
    outMoran <- loadBalanceBplapply(unPairsMoran, function(up) {
      Ixys <- vapply(allResVicari[samples], FUN.VALUE = double(3), function(x) {
        if (up %in% rownames(x[["MoransI_sbivar"]]$result)) x[["MoransI_sbivar"]]$result[up, 1:3] else rep(NA, 3)
      })
      apply(Ixys, 1, function(y) {
        id <- !is.na(y)
        fitMoran <- try(summary(lm(y[id] ~ indivMouse[names(gamCors)[id]]))$coef["(Intercept)", c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")], silent = TRUE)
        if (inherits(fitMoran, "try-error")) {
          fitMoran <- try(summary(lm(y[id] ~ 1))$coef["(Intercept)", c("Estimate", "Pr(>|t|)")])
        }
        fitMoran
      })
    })
    list("GAM" = gamRes, "MoransI_sbivar" = outMoran)
  })
  save(vicariMulti, file = realFitVicariMultiFile)
} else {
  load(realFitVicariMultiFile)
}
vicariMulti2 <- lapply(vicariMulti, function(x) {
  rownames(x$GAM)[2] <- "pVal"
  x$GAM <- t(rbind(x$GAM, "pAdj" = p.adjust(x$GAM["pVal", ], method = "BH"), "qVal" = fdrtoolQval(x$GAM["pVal", ])))[order(x$GAM["pVal", ]), ]
  moransI <- simplify2array(x$MoransI_sbivar)
  cauchyP <- apply(moransI["Pr(>|t|)", , ], 2, sbivar:::CCT)
  x$MoransI_sbivar <- cbind(t(moransI["Estimate", , ]), "pVal" = cauchyP, "pAdj" = p.adjust(cauchyP, method = "BH"))[order(cauchyP), ]
  x
})
rm(vicariMulti)
foo <- gc()
pValsAdjVicari <- lapply(allResVicari, function(x) {
  pvalsAdjGAMMoran <- lapply(x[c("GAM", "MoransI_sbivar")], function(y) {
    tmp <- y$result[, "pAdj"]
    names(tmp) <- rownames(y$result)
    tmp
  })
  names(pvalsAdjGAMMoran)[2] <- "Moran's I\nsbivar"
  qvalsAdjOther <- lapply(x[c("MoranResPerm", "MoranRandomShift")], fdrtoolQval)
  names(qvalsAdjOther) <- c("Moran's I\npermutation", "Moran's I\nRandom shift")
  c(pvalsAdjGAMMoran, qvalsAdjOther)
})
smallestVicariNeg <- simplify2array(Filter(f = is.character, lapply(allResVicari, function(yy) {
  zz <- yy$GAM$result[yy$GAM$result[, "corxy"] < 0, , drop = FALSE]
  if (any(zz[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    rownames(zz)[which.min(zz[, "pAdj"])]
  }
})))
smallestVicariNegMoran <- t(sapply(names(smallestVicariNeg), function(yy) {
  allResVicari[[yy]]$MoransI_sbivar$result[smallestVicariNeg[[yy]], , drop = FALSE]
}))
smalVicGAMPos <- simplify2array(Filter(f = is.character, lapply(allResVicari, function(yy) {
  zz <- yy$GAM$result[yy$GAM$result[, "corxy"] > 0, ]
  if (any(zz[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    rownames(zz)[which.min(zz[, "pAdj"])]
  }
})))
smallestVicariPosMoran <- t(sapply(names(smalVicGAMPos), function(yy) {
  allResVicari[[yy]]$MoransI_sbivar$result[smalVicGAMPos[[yy]], , drop = FALSE]
}))
nor <- base::do.call(rbind, Filter(f = is.data.frame, lapply(SNsamples, function(sn) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result), "Ier5__X690.27454")
  baa <- allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result[tmp, , drop = FALSE]
  colnames(baa)[c(3, 5)] <- c("Correlation", "P.value")
  if (length(tmp)) data.frame("Sample" = sn, baa[, c("Correlation", "P.value"), drop = FALSE])
})))
csrp1 <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sn) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result), "Csrp1__X400.342015")
  baa <- allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result[tmp, , drop = FALSE]
  colnames(baa)[c(3, 5)] <- c("Correlation", "P.value")
  if (length(tmp)) data.frame("Sample" = sn, baa[, c("Correlation", "P.value"), drop = FALSE])
}))
sparc <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(sn) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result), "Sparc__X698.49196")
  baa <- allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result[tmp, , drop = FALSE]
  colnames(baa)[c(3, 5)] <- c("Correlation", "P.value")
  if (length(tmp)) data.frame("Sample" = sn, baa[, c("Correlation", "P.value"), drop = FALSE])
}))
moranCSRp1 <- sapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(yy) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(allResVicari[[yy]]$GAM$result), "Csrp1__X400.342015")
  allResVicari[[yy]]$MoransI_sbivar$result[tmp, ]
})
moranier5 <- base::do.call(rbind, lapply(sampleNamesMouse, function(yy) {
  tmp <- intersect(rownames(allResVicari[[yy]]$GAM$result), "Ier5__X690.27454")
  allResVicari[[yy]]$MoransI_sbivar$result[tmp, ]
}))
smallestVicari <- lapply(pValsAdjVicari, function(yy) {
  lapply(yy, function(zz) {
    if (min(zz, na.rm = TRUE) < sigLevel) {
      names(zz)[which.min(zz)]
    }
  })
})
smallMoranVic <- sapply(smallestVicari, function(x) x[["Moran's I\nsbivar"]])
snFindings <- lapply(SNsamples, function(sn) {
  rownames(allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result)[allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result[, "pAdj"] < sigLevel & allResVicari[[sn]]$GAM$result[, "corxy"] > 0]
})
rm(allResVicari)
fooo <- gc()
```

```{r moranallpaper, include = FALSE}
p <- plotVicariAll(smalVicGAMPos, samples = names(smalVicGAMPos), axisLegendSize = 8.5, legendShift = 8, size = 0.82, stripTextSizeX = 7.8)
ggsave(p, file = "GraphsPaper/PairVicari.pdf", height = 11.5)
gc()
```

\clearpage

#### New findings

The most significant findings are shown in \ref{fig:GAMsVicariNeg}-\ref{fig:MoransIVicari}. The biologically interesting association between \textit{Cspr1} and palmitoyl-L-carnitine is shown in Figure \ref{fig:csrp1Palmitoyl}.

```{r plotGAMAll, fig.cap = "Gene-metabolite pairs with most significant negative correlation according to GAMs in all sections of the Vicari data that have significant negative correlations. None of these findings are significant according to bivariate Moran's I. \\label{fig:GAMsVicariNeg}", fig.height = 8.9}
plotVicariAll(smallestVicariNeg, samples = names(smallestVicariNeg))
rm(smallestVicariNeg)
```

```{r plotMoranAll, fig.cap = "Gene-metabolite pairs most significant according to Moran's I in all sections of the Vicari data. Bivariate Moran's I captures many very short-range associations of genes with metabolite hotspots, but this may also indicate lack of robustness to heteroskedasticity.\\label{fig:MoransIVicari}", fig.height = 8.8}
plotVicariAll(simplify2array(smallMoranVic[sapply(smallMoranVic, is.character)]), samples = names(smallMoranVic), axisLegendSize = 7, stripTextSizeX = 5.4, size = 0.25, xLimShift = 0, legendShift = -160)
rm(smallMoranVic, pValsAdjVicari)
```

\clearpage

```{r plotcsrp1Palmitoyl, fig.cap = paste0("\\textit{Csrp1} transcript and palmitoyl-L-carnitine in striatum samples of the Vicari data (rows). This metabolite is only present in these two sections, where it is highly positively associated with Csrp1, with GAM correlation estimates ", signif(csrp1["V11L12-038_A1", "Correlation"], 3) ," and ",signif(csrp1["V11L12-038_B1", "Correlation"], 3) ," and corresponding p-values ", signif(csrp1["V11L12-038_A1", "P.value"], 2)," and ", signif(csrp1["V11L12-038_B1", "P.value"], 2) ," in samples V11L12-038\\_A1 and V11L12-038\\_B1, respectively. The associations are also significant according to bivariate Moran's I.\\label{fig:csrp1Palmitoyl}"), fig.height = 4}
plotVicariSingle("Csrp1", "X400.342015", samples = sampleNamesMouse[1:2], size = 1)
```

```{r plotIer5, include = FALSE, fig.cap = paste0("\\textit{Ier5} transcript and norepinephrine in substantia nigra samples of the Vicari data (rows). with GAM correlation estimates ", signif(nor["V11T16-085_A1", "Correlation"], 3) ,", ",  signif(nor["V11T16-085_B1", "Correlation"], 3), " and ",signif(nor["V11T16-085_C1", "Correlation"], 3) ," and corresponding p-values ", signif(nor["V11T16-085_A1", "P.value"], 2),", ", signif(nor["V11T16-085_B1", "P.value"], 2), " and ", signif(nor["V11T16-085_C1", "P.value"], 2) ," in samples V11T16-085\\_A1, V11T16-085\\_B1 and V11T16-085\\_C1, respectively. None of these pairs is significant according to bivariate Moran's I.\\label{fig:ier5ne}"), fig.height = 5}
plotVicariSingle("Ier5", "X690.27454", samples = SNsamples, size = 0.6)
```

\clearpage

### Multi-image analysis

We perform a multi-image analysis separately for the striatum and the substantia nigra sections, with a random effect for mouse for the former samples. The p-value distributions are shown in \fref{fig:pvalVicariRep}.

```{r vicariP, fig.cap = "P-value distributions of replicated analysis of GAMs and Moran's I for striatum and substantia nigra samples for GAMs and bivariate Moran's I (plot titles) in the Vicari data\\label{fig:pvalVicariRep}."}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
foo <- lapply(vicariGroups, function(vg) {
  lapply(selfName(c("GAM", "MoransI_sbivar")), function(meth) {
    hist(vicariMulti2[[vg]][[meth]][, "pVal"], main = paste0(switch(vg,
      "SN" = "Substantia nigra",
      "Stria" = "Striatum"
    ), "\n", switch(meth,
      "GAM" = "GAMs",
      "MoransI_sbivar" = "Moran's I"
    )), xlab = "P-value")
  })
})
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
rm(vicariMulti, vicariMulti2)
```

# Recommendations

\begin{table}
\footnotesize
\begin{tabularx}{1.0\textwidth}{|>{\setlength\hsize{0.82\hsize}\setlength\linewidth{\hsize}}X|>{\setlength\hsize{0.9\hsize}\setlength\linewidth{\hsize}}X|>{\setlength\hsize{1.28\hsize}\setlength\linewidth{\hsize}}X|}
\hline
Method & Strengths & Weaknesses \\
\hline
Methods with permutation or randomisation null distributions: \textit{SpaGene}, \textit{SpatialDM}, \textit{LIANA+}, Geary's C, Lee's L some versions of bivariate Moran's I &
\begin{itemize}
\item Fast
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item{Lee's L requires spot matching for differing resolutions}
\item {Lak robustness to SAC, leading to spurious significances for bivariate associations}
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Pearson correlation  & 
\begin{itemize}
\item{Fast}
\item{Intuitive interpretation}
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
 \item{Requires spot matching for differing resolutions}
 \item{Yields spurious significances if SAC is present in both variables}
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Modified t-test &
\begin{itemize}
\item{Intuitive interpretation: the quantity of interest is Pearson correlation}
\item{Robust to many types of SAC}
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item{Requires spot matching for differing resolutions}
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Random shift test &
\begin{itemize}
\item Applicable to any measure of spatial association
\item Preserves SAC in permutation test
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item Computationally demanding
\item The p-values are discretized by the number of random shifts
\item Limited power as some spatial association is retained in the random shifts
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Bivariate Moran's I [sbivar] &
\begin{itemize}
\item Nonparametric
\item Intuitive interpretation: closest equivalent of Pearson correlation under disjoint coordinates
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item Weight matrix needs to be user defined, determining sensitivity to different types of spatial patterns
\item Affected by heteroskedasticity
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Bivariate Gaussian processes (GPs) [sbivar] &
\begin{itemize}
\item Robust to many types of SAC
\item Amendable to other outcome distributions and link functions
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item Very high computational demands
\item Does not provide a measure of bivariate association strength, only a p-value
\item Relies on parametric assumptions
\item Complex mathematical derivation
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
Generalized additive models (GAMs) [sbivar] &
\begin{itemize}
\item Easily amendable to other outcome distributions and link functions
\item Intuitive interpretation and visualisation
\item Good power to detect moderate to long range differences
\end{itemize} &
\begin{itemize}
\item Low power to detect short-range bivariate spatial association
\item Struggles with hotspots and edge effects
\item Relies on parametric assumptions
\item Version accounting for residual SAC in a GAMM is very slow
\end{itemize}\\
\hline
\end{tabularx}
\caption{\label{tab:overview}Methods to test for bivariate spatial association considered in this study, with strengths and weaknesses. [sbivar] indicates methods proposed or improved in this work and available in the \sbivar R-package.} 
\end{table}

\clearpage

# Software versions

```{r sessionInfo}
sessionInfo()
```

\printbibliography
